title: A Private Conversation
titleTranslate: 為什麼作者會向他身後的人抱怨呢？
text:
Why did the writer complain to the people behind him?
Last week I went to the theatre.
I had a very good seat.
The play was very interesting.
I did not enjoy it.
A young man and a young woman were sitting behind me.
They were talking loudly.
I got very angry.
I could not hear the actors.
I turned round.
I looked at the man and the woman angrily.
They did not pay any attention.
In the end, I could not bear it.
I turned round again.
'I can't hear a word!' I said angrily.
'It's none of your business,'
the young man said rudely.
'This is a private conversation!'
textTranslate:
為什麼作者會向他身後的人抱怨呢？
上週我去劇院看戲。
我的座位非常好。
這出戏非常有趣。
我並不喜歡它。
一個年輕男子和一個年輕女子坐在我後面。
他們說話的聲音很大。
我非常生氣。
我聽不到演員們的聲音。
我轉過身來。
我憤怒地看著那個男人和那個女人。
他們根本沒有理會。
最後，我實在無法忍受下去了。
我又轉過身來。
“我一個字都聽不見！”我憤怒地說道。
“這不關你的事。”
那個年輕人粗魯地說道。
“這可是私人談話！”

title: Breakfast or Lunch
titleTranslate: 為什麼作者的阿姨會感到驚訝呢？
text:
Why was the writer's aunt surprised?
It was Sunday.
I never get up early on Sundays.
I sometimes stay in bed until lunchtime.
Last Sunday I got up very late.
I looked out of the window.
It was dark outside.
'What a day!' I thought.
'It's raining again.'
Just then, the telephone rang.
It was my aunt Lucy.
'I've just arrived by train,' she said.
'I'm coming to see you.'
'But I'm still having breakfast,' I said.
'What are you doing?' she asked.
'I'm having breakfast,' I repeated.
'Dear me,' she said.
'Do you always get up so late?
It's one o'clock!'
textTranslate:
為什麼作者的阿姨會感到驚訝呢？
那是星期天。
我從來不在週日早起。
有時候我會一直躺在床上，直到午餐時間才起床。
上週日，我起得很晚。
我望向了窗外。
外面一片漆黑。
“真是美好的一天啊！”我想。
“又下雨了。”
就在這時，電話響了。
那是我的阿姨露西。
“我剛乘火車到達的，”她說。
“我來看你了。”
“但我還在吃早餐呢，”我說。
“你在做什麼？”她問。
“我在吃早餐，”我重複道。
“天哪，”她說。
“你總是起這麼晚嗎？
現在都一點鐘了！”

title: Please Send Me a Card
titleTranslate: 作者寄了多少張明信片？
text:
How many cards did the writer send?
Postcards always spoil my holidays.
Last summer, I went to Italy.
I visited museums and sat in public gardens.
A friendly waiter taught me a few words of Italian.
Then he lent me a book.
I read a few lines, but I did not understand a word.
Everyday I thought about postcards.
My holidays passed quickly,
but I did not send cards to my friends.
On the last day I made a big decision.
I got up early and bought thirty-seven cards.
I spent the whole day in my room,
but I did not write a single card!
textTranslate:
作者寄了多少張明信片？
明信片總是把我的假期攪得一團糟。
去年夏天，我去了義大利。
我參觀了博物館，還坐在公共花園裡休息。
一位友好的服務員教了我幾句義大利語。
之後他還借給我一本書。
我讀了幾行，但一個詞兒也不懂。
我每天都想著明信片的事。
假期很快就過去了，
但我卻沒有給朋友們寄任何卡片。
在最後一天，我下了一個大決心。
我早早起床，買了三十七張明信片。
我一整天都待在房間裡，
卻一張卡片都沒寫！

title: An Exciting Trip
titleTranslate: 為什麼蒂姆覺得這次旅行如此令人興奮呢？
text:
Why is Tim finding this trip exciting?
I have just received a letter from my brother, Tim.
He is in Australia.
He has been there for six months.
Tim is an engineer.
He is working for a big firm and he has already visited
a great number of different places in Australia.
He has just bought an Australian car and has gone to Alice Springs,
a small town in the centre of Australia.
He will soon visit Darwin.
From there, he will fly to Perth.
My brother has never been abroad before,
so he is finding this trip very exciting.
textTranslate:
為什麼蒂姆覺得這次旅行如此令人興奮呢？
我剛剛收到了我哥哥蒂姆的一封信。
他在澳大利亞。
他已經在那裡待了六個月了。
蒂姆是一名工程師。
他在一家大公司工作，並且已經去過澳大利亞的許多不同地方。
他剛買了一輛澳大利亞產的汽車，然後去了愛麗絲泉（Alice Springs）。
澳大利亞中部的一個小鎮。
他很快將會去達爾文。
從那裡，他將飛往珀斯。
我哥哥以前從未出過國。
所以他覺得這次旅行非常令人興奮。

title: No Wrong Numbers
titleTranslate: “沒有錯誤的數字”是什麼意思？
text:
What does 'No wrong numbers' mean?
Mr.James Scott has a garage in Silbury
and now he has just bought another garage in Pinhurst.
Pinhurst is only five miles from Silbury,
but Mr. Scott cannot get a telephone for his new garage,
so he has just bought twelve pigeons.
Yesterday, a pigeon carried the first message from Pinhurst to Silbury.
The bird covered the distance in three minutes.
Up to now,
Mr.Scott has sent a great many requests for spare parts
and other urgent messages from one garage to the other.
In this way, he has begun his own private 'telephone' service.
textTranslate:
“沒有錯誤的數字”是什麼意思？
詹姆斯·斯科特先生在塞爾伯裡（Silbury）有一間車庫。
現在他又在 Pinhurst 買了一個車庫。
平赫斯特（Pinhurst）距離西爾伯裡（Silbury）只有五英里。
但是斯科特先生無法為他的新車庫配備一部電話。
所以他剛剛買了十二隻鴿子。
昨天，一隻鴿子把第一封信從平赫斯特送到了塞爾伯裡。
這隻鴿子花了三分鐘飛完了這段路程。
到目前為止，
斯科特先生已經從一個車庫向另一個車庫傳送了大量索取備件的請求
以及其他緊急資訊。
透過這種方式，他開始了自己的私人“電話”服務。

title: Percy Buttons
titleTranslate: 珀西·巴頓斯（Percy Buttons）是誰？
text:
Who is Percy Buttons?
I have just moved to a house in Bridge Street.
Yesterday a beggar knocked at my door.
He asked me for a meal and a glass of beer.
In return for this,
the beggar stood on his head and sang songs.
I gave him a meal.
He ate the food and drank the beer.
Then he put a piece of cheese in his pocket and went away.
Later a neighbour told me about him.
Everybody knows him.
His name is Percy Buttons.
He calls at every house in the street once a month
and always asks for a meal and a glass of beer.
textTranslate:
珀西·巴頓斯（Percy Buttons）是誰？
我剛剛搬到了 Bridge Street 上的一棟房子裡。
昨天，一個乞丐敲了我的門。
他向我要了一頓飯和一杯啤酒。
作為對此的回報，
那個乞丐倒立著，同時還在唱歌。
我給他準備了一頓飯。
他吃了食物，喝了啤酒。
然後他把一塊乳酪放進口袋裡，就離開了。
後來，一位鄰居跟我講起了他的情況。
每個人都知道他。
他的名字叫珀西·巴頓斯（Percy Buttons）。
他每個月都會挨家挨戶地拜訪這條街上的每一戶人家。
並且總是要求吃一頓飯和喝一杯啤酒。

title: Too Late
titleTranslate: 偵探們保住那些鑽石了嗎？
text:
Did the detectives save the diamonds?
The plane was late and detectives were waiting at the airport all morning.
They were expecting a valuable parcel of diamonds from South Africa.
A few hours earlier,
someone had told the police that
thieves would try to steal the diamonds.
When the plane arrived,
some of the detectives were waiting
inside the main building
while others were waiting on the airfield.
Two men took the parcel off the plane
and carried it into the Customs House.
While two detectives were keeping guard at the door,
two others opened the parcel.
To their surprise,
the precious parcel was full of stones and sand!
textTranslate:
偵探們保住那些鑽石了嗎？
飛機晚點了，偵探們一整個上午都在機場等待。
他們正在等待一個從南非來的裝有鑽石的貴重包裹。
幾個小時之前，
有人告訴警方說
小偷會企圖偷走那些鑽石。
當飛機降臨時，
一些偵探正在等待。
在主樓內
而其他人則在停機坪上等待。
兩名男子從飛機上取下了那個包裹。
然後把它搬進了海關大樓。
當兩名偵探在門口守衛時，
另外兩名偵探開啟了那個包裹。
令他們驚訝的是，
那個貴重的包裹裡裝滿了石頭和沙子！

title: The Best and the Worst
titleTranslate: 為什麼喬的花園是
text:
Why is Joe's garden
the most beautiful one in the town?
Joe Sanders has the most beautiful garden
in our town.
Nearly everybody enters for
'The Nicest Garden Competition' each year,
but Joe wins every time.
Bill Frith's garden is larger than Joe's.
Bill works harder than Joe
and grows more flowers and vegetables,
but Joe's garden is more interesting.
He has made neat paths
and has built a wooden bridge over a pool.
I like gardens too,
but I do not like hard work.
Every year
I enter for the garden competition too,
and I always win a little prize
for the worst garden in the town!
textTranslate:
為什麼喬的花園是
鎮上最漂亮的那一個？
喬·桑德斯擁有最美麗的花園。
在我們鎮上。
幾乎每個人都參加
“最美麗花園競賽”
但是喬每次都能贏。
比爾·弗里斯的花園比喬的大。
比爾比喬工作更努力。
並且能種植出更多的鮮花和蔬菜。
不過喬的花園更有趣。
他修了整潔的小路
還在池塘上建了一座木橋。
我也喜歡花園。
但我不喜歡辛苦的工作。
每年
我也會參加花園比賽。
而且我總能贏得一個小獎品。
因為鎮上最差的花園！

title: A Cold Welcome
titleTranslate: “冷遇”（a cold welcome）指的是一種不熱情、冷漠或甚至帶有敵意的接待方式。
text:
What does 'a cold welcome' refer to?
On Wednesday evening, we went to the Town Hall.
It was the last day of the year
and a large crowd of people had gathered under the Town Hall clock.
It would strike twelve in twenty minutes' time.
Fifteen minutes passed and then,
at five to twelve, the clock stopped.
The big minute hand did not move.
We waited and waited,
but nothing happened.
Suddenly someone shouted.
'It's two minutes past twelve!
The clock has stopped!'
I looked at my watch.
It was true.
The big clock refused to welcome the New Year.
At that moment, everybody began to laugh and sing.
textTranslate:
“冷遇”（a cold welcome）指的是一種不熱情、冷漠或甚至帶有敵意的接待方式。
週三晚上，我們去了市政廳。
那是這一年中的最後一天。
一大群人聚集在市政廳的鐘樓下方。
二十分鐘後，鐘聲就會敲響十二下。
十五分鐘過去了，然後……
在十二點差五分的時候，鐘停了。
那個大型的分針一動也沒有動。
我們等啊等啊，
但是什麼事情都沒有發生。
突然，有人喊了一聲。
已經十二點過兩分了！
“時鐘停了！”
我看了看我的手錶。
確實如此。
那座大鐘拒絕迎接新年。
就在那一刻，所有人都開始大笑起來，還唱起了歌。

title: Not for Jazz
titleTranslate: 那架擊弦古鋼琴後來怎麼樣了？
text:
What happened to the clavichord?
We have an old musical instrument.
It is called a clavichord.
It was made in Germany in 1681.
Our clavichord is kept in the living room.
It has belonged to our family for a long time.
The instrument was bought by my grandfather many years ago.
Recently it was damaged by a visitor.
She tried to play jazz on it!
She struck the keys too hard and two of the strings were broken.
My father was shocked.
Now we are not allowed to touch it.
It is being repaired by a friend of my father's.
textTranslate:
那架擊弦古鋼琴後來怎麼樣了？
我們有一件古老的樂器。
它叫擊弦古鋼琴。
它於1681年在德國製造。
我們的擊弦古鋼琴放在客廳裡。
它已經屬於我們家族很長時間了。
這件樂器是我祖父多年前買的。
最近，它被一位訪客損壞了。
她竟然用它彈起了爵士樂！
她用力過猛地敲擊琴鍵，結果有兩根琴絃斷裂了。
我父親非常震驚。
現在我們不準碰它了。
我父親的一個朋友正在修理它。

title: One Good Turn Deserves Another
titleTranslate: 誰付了託尼晚餐的錢？
text:
Who paid for Tony's dinner?
I was having dinner at a restaurant when Tony Steele came in.
Tony worked in a lawyer's office years ago,
but he is now working at a bank.
He gets a good salary,
but he always borrows money from his friends and never pays it back.
Tony saw me and came and sat at the same table.
He has never borrowed money from me.
While he was eating, I asked him to lend me twenty pounds.
To my surprise, he gave me the money immediately.
'I have never borrowed any money from you,'
Tony said, 'so now you can pay for my dinner!'
textTranslate:
誰付了託尼晚餐的錢？
我正在餐廳吃晚飯，這時託尼·斯蒂爾走了進來。
託尼幾年前在一家律師事務所工作過。
但他現在在一家銀行工作。
他的薪水很高。
但他總是向朋友借錢，卻從不還錢。
託尼看到我後，走過來坐在了我的旁邊。
他從來沒有向我借過錢。
他正在吃飯的時候，我請他借給我二十英鎊。
令我驚訝的是，他立刻就把錢給了我。
“我從來沒有向你借過任何錢。”
託尼說：“現在你可以付我晚餐的錢了！”

title: Goodbye and Good Luck
titleTranslate: 艾莉森上尉要去哪裡？怎麼去？
text:
Where is Captain Alison going and how?
Our neighbour, Captain Charles Alison, will sail from Portsmouth tomorrow.
We'll meet him at the harbour early in the morning.
He will be in his small boat, Topsail.
Topsail is a famous little boat.
It has sailed across the Atlantic many times.
Captain Alison will set out at eight o'clock,
so we'll have plenty of time.
We'll see his boat and then we'll say goodbye to him.
He will be away for two months.
We are very proud of him.
He will take part in an important race across the Atlantic.
textTranslate:
艾莉森上尉要去哪裡？怎麼去？
我們的鄰居查爾斯·艾利森上尉明天將從樸次茅斯啟航。
我們將在清晨在港口與他見面。
他會待在他的小船'託普賽爾'號上。
“Topsail”是一艘非常著名的小船。
它已經多次橫渡了大西洋。
艾莉森船長八點就要出發了，
所以我們會有足夠的時間。
我們會去看看他的船，然後跟他告別。
他將會離開兩個月。
我們為他感到非常驕傲。
他將參加一場橫跨大西洋的重要比賽。

title: The Greenwood Boys
titleTranslate: 為什麼警察會遇到困難呢？
text:
Why will the police have a difficult time?
The Greenwood Boys are a group of pop singers.
At present, they are visiting all parts of the country.
They will be arriving here tomorrow.
They will be coming by train and most of the young people in the town will be meeting them at the station.
Tomorrow evening they will be singing at the Workers' Club.
The Greenwood Boys will be staying for five days.
During this time, they will give five performances.
As usual, the police will have a difficult time.
They will be trying to keep order.
It is always the same on these occasions.
textTranslate:
為什麼警察會遇到困難呢？
The Greenwood Boys是一支流行歌手組合。
目前，他們正在全國巡迴演出。
他們明天會到達這裡。
他們將乘火車前來，鎮上的大多數年輕人都會在火車站迎接他們。
明晚他們將在工人俱樂部演出。
格林伍德男孩們將在此逗留五天。
在這段時間裡，他們將進行五場演出。
像往常一樣，警方會遇到很大的困難。
他們會努力維持秩序。
每逢這種場合，情況總是如此。

title: Do You Speak English
titleTranslate: 那個年輕人會說英語嗎？
text:
Did the young man speak English?
I had an amusing experience last year.
After I had left a small village in the south of France,
I drove on to the next town.
On the way, a young man waved to me.
I stopped and he asked me for a lift.
As soon as he had got into the car,
I said good morning to him in French and he replied in the same language.
Apart from a few words,
I do not know any French at all.
Neither of us spoke during the journey.
I had nearly reached the town, when the young man suddenly said, very slowly,
'Do you speak English?'
As I soon learnt, he was English himself!
textTranslate:
那個年輕人會說英語嗎？
去年我有一段非常有趣的經歷。
我繼續開車駛向下一個城鎮。
我繼續開車前往下一個城鎮。
路上，一個年輕人向我招手。
我停下車，他問我能否搭個便車。
他一上車，
我用法語向他道了早安，他也用法語回應了我。
除了幾個單詞之外，
我完全不會說法語。
在旅途中，我們倆都沒有說話。
我快到鎮上的時候，那個年輕人突然非常緩慢地說：
“你會說英語嗎？”
很快我就發現，他本人就是英國人！

title: Good News
titleTranslate: 那條好訊息是什麼呢？
text:
What was the good news?
The secretary told me that Mr. Harmsworth would see me.
I felt very nervous when I went into his office.
He did not look up from his desk when I entered.
After I had sat down,
he said that business was very bad.
He told me that the firm could not afford to pay such large salaries.
Twenty people had already left.
I knew that my turn had come.
'Mr. Harmsworth,' I said in a weak voice.
'Don't interrupt,' he said.
Then he smiled and told me I would receive an extra thousand pounds a year!
textTranslate:
那條好訊息是什麼呢？
秘書告訴我，哈姆斯沃思先生會見我。
當我走進他的辦公室時，我感到非常緊張。
我進去的時候，他頭也沒抬，眼睛一直盯著書桌。
在我坐下之後，
他說生意非常糟糕。
他告訴我，公司無力支付如此高的薪水。
已經有二十個人離開了。
我知道輪到我了。
“哈姆斯沃思先生，”我虛弱地說道。
“不要打斷我，”他說。
然後他笑了，告訴我每年還能多拿一千英鎊！

title: A Polite Request
titleTranslate: 那個禮貌的請求是什麼？
text:
What was the polite request?
If you park your car in the wrong place,
a traffic policeman will soon find it.
You will be very lucky if he lets you go without a ticket.
However, this does not always happen.
Traffic police are sometimes very polite.
During a holiday in Sweden,
I found this note on my car:
'sir, we welcome you to our city. This is a No Parking area.
You will enjoy your stay here if you pay attention to our street signs. This note is only a reminder.'
If you receive a request like this, you cannot fail to obey it!
textTranslate:
那個禮貌的請求是什麼？
如果你把車停在了錯誤的地方，
交通警察很快就會發現它。
如果你能不被開罰單就放你走，那你就非常幸運了。
不過，這種情況並不總是發生。
有時候，交通警察會非常禮貌。
在瑞典的一個假期裡，
我在我的車上發現了一張紙條：
“先生，歡迎您來到我們的城市。這裡是禁止停車區。
如果您注意看街道上的標誌，您的停留會非常愉快。這張紙條只是為了提醒您而已。”
如果你收到這樣的請求，你一定會遵守的！

title: Always Young
titleTranslate: 為什麼詹妮弗阿姨從不告訴別人她的真實年齡呢？
text:
Why doesn't Aunt Jennifer tell anyone how old she is?
My aunt Jennifer is an actress.
She must be at least thirty-five years old.
In spite of this, she often appears on the stage as a young girl.
Jennifer will have to take part in a new play soon.
This time, she will be a girl of seventeen.
In the play, she must appear in a bright red dress and long black stockings.
Last year in another play,
she had to wear short socks and a bright, orange-coloured dress.
If anyone ever asks her how old she is,
she always answers, 'Darling, it must be terrible to be grown up!'
textTranslate:
為什麼詹妮弗阿姨從不告訴別人她的真實年齡呢？
我的阿姨詹妮弗是一名演員。
她至少有三十五歲了。
儘管如此，她仍然經常以年輕女孩的形象出現在舞臺上。
詹妮弗很快將出演一部新劇。
這一次，她將是一個十七歲的女孩。
在劇中，她必須穿著鮮紅色的連衣裙和黑色長筒襪出場。
去年，在另一部戲劇中……
她不得不穿短襪和一條鮮豔的橙色連衣裙。
如果有人問她多大了，
她總是回答：“親愛的，長大一定很可怕吧！”

title: He Often does This
titleTranslate: 作者的包發生了什麼事？
text:
What had happened to the writer's bag?
After I had had lunch at a village pub, I looked for my bag.
I had left it on a chair beside the door and now it wasn't there!
As I was looking for it, the landlord came in.
'Did you have a good meal?' he asked.
'Yes, thank you,' I answered, 'but I can't pay the bill. I haven't got my bag.'
The landlord smiled and immediately went out.
In a few minutes he returned with my bag and gave it back to me.
'I'm very sorry,' he said.
'My dog had taken it into the garden. He often does this!'
textTranslate:
作者的包發生了什麼事？
在一家鄉村酒吧吃過午飯後，我開始尋找我的包。
我之前把它放在門旁邊的椅子上，但現在它不見了！
我正在找包的時候，店主進來了。
“你吃得怎麼樣？”他問道。
“是的，謝謝您，”我回答道，“但我付不了賬單。我的包不見了。”
店主笑了笑，馬上就出去了。
幾分鐘後，他拿著我的包回來了，並把它交還給了我。
“我非常抱歉，”他說。
“我的狗把它叼到花園裡去了。它老是幹這種事！”

title: Sold Out
titleTranslate: 作者什麼時候能看到這部劇呢？
text:
When will the writer see the play?
'The play may begin at any moment,' I said.
'It may have begun already,' Susan answered.
I hurried to the ticket office.
'May I have two tickets please?' I asked.
'I'm sorry, we've sold out,' the girl said.
'What a pity!' Susan exclaimed.
Just then, a man hurried to the ticket office.
'Can I return these two tickets?' he asked.
'Certainly,' the girl said.
I went back to the ticket office at once.
'Could I have those two tickets please?' I asked.
'Certainly,' the girl said,
'but they're for next Wednesday's performance.
Do you still want them?'
'I might as well have them,' I said sadly.
textTranslate:
作者什麼時候能看到這部劇呢？
“演出隨時可能開始，”我說。
“可能已經開始了，”蘇珊回答。
我趕緊跑向售票處。
“請給我兩張票，好嗎？”我問。
“很抱歉，票已經賣完了，”女孩說。
“真遺憾！”蘇珊感嘆道。
就在這時，一個男人匆匆跑到了售票處。
“我可以退掉這兩張票嗎？”他問道。
“當然可以，”女孩回答。
我立刻又回到了售票處。
“請把那兩張票給我，好嗎？”我問。
“當然可以，”女孩說道。
但這是下週三演出的票。
你還要嗎？
“那我就要了吧，”我沮喪地說。

title: One Man in a Boat
titleTranslate: 為什麼釣魚是這位作家最喜愛的運動呢？
text:
Why is fishing the writer's favourite sport?
Fishing is my favourite sport.
I often fish for hours without catching anything.
But this does not worry me.
Some fishermen are unlucky.
Instead of catching fish,
they catch old boots and rubbish.
I am even less lucky.
I never catch anything  --  not even old boots.
After having spent whole mornings on the river,
I always go home with an empty bag.
'You must give up fishing!' my friends say. 'It's a waste of time.'
But they don't realize one important thing.
I'm not really interested in fishing.
I am only interested in sitting in a boat and doing nothing at all!
textTranslate:
為什麼釣魚是這位作家最喜愛的運動呢？
釣魚是我最喜歡的運動。
我經常花好幾個小時釣魚，卻什麼也沒釣到。
但這並不讓我擔心。
有些漁民運氣不好。
與其去捕魚，
他們收集舊靴子和垃圾。
我的運氣更差了。
我從來什麼也抓不到——連一雙舊靴子都抓不到。
在河邊度過了整個上午之後……
我每次回家時，包裡總是空空的。
“你必須放棄釣魚！”我的朋友們說，“那純粹是浪費時間。”
但他們沒有意識到一件重要的事情：
我其實對釣魚並不感興趣。
我真正感興趣的，只是坐在船上什麼都不做而已！

title: Mad or Not
titleTranslate: 為什麼人們認為這位作者瘋了？
text:
Why do people think the writer is mad?
Aeroplanes are slowly driving me mad.
I live near an airport and passing planes can be heard night and day.
The airport was built years ago, but for some reason it could not be used then.
Last year, however, it came into use.
Over a hundred people must have been driven away from their homes by the noise.
I am one of the few people left.
Sometimes I think this house will be knocked down by a passing plane.
I have been offered a large sum of money to go away, but I am determined to stay here.
Everybody says I must be mad and they are probably right.
textTranslate:
為什麼人們認為這位作者瘋了？
飛機正慢慢地把我逼瘋。
我住在機場附近，無論白天還是黑夜，都能聽到飛機的轟鳴聲。
這個機場幾年前就建好了，但不知為何當時無法投入使用。
然而去年，它終於開始運營了。
肯定有一百多人被噪音逼得離開了家。
我是少數還留在這裡的人之一。
有時候我覺得這房子會被飛過的飛機撞倒。
有人出高價讓我搬走，但我堅決要留在這裡。
大家都說我一定是瘋了，也許他們是對的。

title: A Glass Envelope
titleTranslate: 簡是怎麼收到一封來自陌生人的信的？
text:
How did Jane receive a letter from a stranger?
My daughter, Jane, never dreamed of receiving a letter from a girl of her own age in Holland.
Last year, we were travelling across the Channel and Jane put a piece of paper with her name and address on it into a bottle.
She threw the bottle into the sea.
She never thought of it again, but ten months later, she received a letter from a girl in Holland.
Both girls write to each other regularly now.
However, they have decided to use the post office.
Letters will cost a little more, but they will certainly travel faster.
textTranslate:
簡是怎麼收到一封來自陌生人的信的？
我的女兒簡，從未想過會收到來自荷蘭的一個同齡女孩的信。
去年，我們在渡英吉利海峽時，簡把一張寫有她名字和地址的紙條放進了瓶子裡。
她把瓶子扔進了海里。
她再也沒有想起過這件事，但十個月後，她收到了一封來自荷蘭女孩的信。
現在，這兩個女孩經常互相寫信。
然而，她們決定用郵局寄信。
信件的費用會稍高一些，但它們的傳遞速度肯定更快。

title: A New House
titleTranslate: 為什麼這棟新房子這麼特別呢？
text:
Why is the new house special?
I had a letter from my sister yesterday.
She lives in Nigeria.
In her letter, she said that she would come to England next year.
If she comes, she will get a surprise.
We are now living in a beautiful new house in the country.
Work on it had begun before my sister left.
The house was completed five months ago.
In my letter, I told her that she could stay with us.
The house has many large rooms and there is a lovely garden.
It is a very modern house, so it looks strange to some people.
It must be the only modern house in the district.
textTranslate:
為什麼這棟新房子這麼特別呢？
我昨天收到了姐姐的一封信。
她住在奈及利亞。
她在信中說，她明年會來英格蘭。
如果她來了，她會收到一個驚喜。
我們現在住在鄉下的一棟漂亮的新房子裡。
我妹妹離開前，這棟房子就已經開始建了。
這棟房子是在五個月前建成的。
我在信裡告訴她，她可以和我們住在一起。
這棟房子有很多寬敞的房間，還有一個非常漂亮的花園。
這是一棟非常現代化的房子，因此有些人會覺得它看起來很奇怪。
這一定是這個地區唯一的一棟現代化房子了。

title: It Could be Worse
titleTranslate: 那位作家的錢被偷了嗎？
text:
Had the writer's money been stolen?
I entered the hotel manager's office and sat down.
I had just lost ￡50 and I felt very upset.
'I left the money in my room,' I said, 'and it's not there now.  '
The manager was sympathetic, but he could do nothing.
'Everyone's losing money these days,' he said.
He started to complain about this wicked world but was interrupted by a knock at the door.
A girl came in and put an envelope on his desk.
It contained ￡50.
'I found this outside this gentleman's room,' she said.
'Well,' I said to the manager,'there is still some honesty in this world!'
textTranslate:
那位作家的錢被偷了嗎？
我走進酒店經理的辦公室，坐了下來。
我剛剛損失了50英鎊，感到非常沮喪。
“我把錢放在房間裡了，但現在錢不見了。”我說。
經理很同情我，但他無能為力。
“如今大家都在丟錢，”他說。
他開始抱怨這個邪惡的世界，但被一陣敲門聲打斷了。
一個女孩走了進來，把一個信封放在他的桌子上。
信封裡裝著50英鎊。
“我在這位先生的房間外面發現了這個。”她說。
“嗯，”我對經理說，“這個世界上還是有一些誠實的人的！”

title: Do the English Speak English
titleTranslate: 他們說英語嗎？
text:
Do they speak English?
Why does the writer not understand the porter?
I arrived in London at last.
The railway station was big, black and dark.
I did not know the way to my hotel, so I asked a porter.
I not only spoke English very carefully, but very clearly as well.
The porter, however, could not understand me.
I repeated my question several times and at last he understood.
he answered me, but he spoke neither slowly nor clearly.
'I am a foreigner,' I said.
Then he spoke slowly, but I could not understand him.
My teacher never spoke English like that!
The porter and I looked at each other and smiled.
Then he said something and I understood it.
'You'll soon learn English!' he said.
I wonder.
In England, each person speaks a different language.
The English understand each other, but I don't understand them!
textTranslate:
他們說英語嗎？
為什麼作者聽不懂那個行李搬運工的話？
我終於到了倫敦。
火車站又大又黑，顯得非常陰暗。
我不知道怎麼去我的酒店，於是我問了一個行李搬運工。
我不僅英語說得很仔細，而且發音也很清晰。
然而，那個行李搬運工卻聽不懂我。
我重複了幾次我的問題，最後他才明白了。
他回答了我，但他說得既不慢也不清楚。
“我是外國人，”我說。
然後他放慢了語速，但我還是聽不懂他。
我的老師從來都不會用那樣的方式說英語！
那個行李搬運工和我對視了一眼，然後都笑了。
接著他說了些什麼，我聽懂了。
“你很快就能學會英語了！”他說。
這可不好說。
在英格蘭，每個人說著不同的語言。
英國人之間可以互相理解，但我卻聽不懂他們的話！

title: The Best Art Critics
titleTranslate: 誰是這位學生最好的評論家？
text:
Who is the student's best critic?
I am an art student and I paint a lot of pictures.
Many people pretend that they understand modern art.
They always tell you what a picture is 'about'.
Of course, many pictures are not 'about' anything.
They are just pretty patterns.
We like them in the same way that we like pretty curtain material.
I think that young children often appreciate modern pictures better than anyone else.
They notice more.
My sister is only seven,
but she always tells me whether my pictures are good or not.
She came into my room yesterday.
'What are you doing?' she asked.
'I'm hanging this picture on the wall,' I answered. 'It's a new one. Do you like it?'
She looked at it critically for a moment.
'It's all right,' she said, 'but isn't it upside down?'
I looked at it again.
She was right! It was!
textTranslate:
誰是這位學生最好的評論家？
我是一名藝術學生，經常畫很多畫。
很多人假裝自己理解現代藝術。
當然，很多畫其實什麼也不是。
當然，很多圖片其實並沒有明確的主題或意義（即它們“並不表達任何特定的內容”）。
它們只不過是一些漂亮的圖案罷了。
我們喜歡它們的方式，就如同我們喜歡漂亮的窗簾面料一樣。
我認為，小孩子往往比任何人都更懂得欣賞現代畫。
他們看到的更多。
我妹妹才七歲。
但她總會告訴我我的畫好不好。
她昨天進了我的房間。
“你在做什麼？”她問。
“我在把這幅畫掛在牆上。”我回答說，“這是一幅新畫。你喜歡嗎？”
她挑剔地看了一會兒。
“還行吧，”她說，“不過它不是倒掛了嗎？”
我又看了一遍。
她說得對！確實是這樣！

title: A Wet Night
titleTranslate: 那些男孩們晚上發生了什麼事？
text:
What happened to the boys in the night?
Late in the afternoon, the boys put up their tent in the middle of a field.
As soon as this was done, they cooked a meal over an open fire.
They were all hungry and the food smelled good.
After a wonderful meal, they told stories and sang songs by the campfire.
But some time later it began to rain.
The boys felt tired so they put out the fire and crept into their tent.
Their sleeping bags were warm and comfortable, so they all slept soundly.
In the middle of the night, two boys woke up and began shouting.
The tent was full of water!
They all leapt out of their sleeping bags and hurried outside.
It was raining heavily and they found that a stream had formed in the field.
The stream wound its way across the field and then flowed right under their tent!
textTranslate:
那些男孩們晚上發生了什麼事？
傍晚時分，男孩們在田野中央搭起了帳篷。
帳篷一搭好，他們就在篝火上做飯了。
大家都很餓，食物聞起來非常香。
吃完美味的飯菜後，他們在篝火旁講故事、唱歌。
但不久之後，天開始下雨了。
男孩們感到累了，於是他們熄滅了篝火，鑽進了帳篷。
他們的睡袋溫暖舒適，大家都睡得很香。
半夜時分，有兩個男孩突然醒來，開始大喊大叫。
帳篷裡充滿了水！
他們一下子從睡袋裡跳出來，趕緊跑到外面。
雨下得很大，他們發現田野裡形成了一條小溪。
小溪蜿蜒流過田野，然後從他們的帳篷下方流過！

title: No Parking
titleTranslate: 賈斯珀·懷特遇到了什麼麻煩？
text:
What is Jasper White's problem?
Jasper White is one of those rare people who believes in ancient myths.
He has just bought a new house in the city, but ever since he moved in, he has had trouble with cars and their owners.
When he returns home at night, he always finds that someone has parked a car outside his gate.
Because of this, he has not been able to get his own car into his garage even once.
Jasper has put up 'No Parking' signs outside his gate, but these have not had any effect.
Now he has put an ugly stone head over the gate.
It is one of the ugliest faces I have ever seen.
I asked him what it was and he told me that it was Medusa, the Gorgon.
Jasper hopes that she will turn cars and their owners to stone.
But none of them has been turned to stone yet!
textTranslate:
賈斯珀·懷特遇到了什麼麻煩？
賈斯珀·懷特是少數相信古代神話的人之一。
他剛在城裡買了一套新房子，但從搬進去之後，他就一直為汽車和車主們的事而煩惱。
每當他晚上回到家時，總會發現有人把車停在了他家大門的外面。
正因為如此，他一次都無法將自己的汽車開進自己的車庫。
賈斯珀在他的大門外豎起了“禁止停車”的標誌，但這些標誌根本沒有任何作用。
現在，他在大門上方安放了一個醜陋的石像頭。
這是我見過的最醜陋的面孔之一。
我問他那是什麼，他告訴我那是蛇髮女妖美杜莎。
賈斯珀希望她能夠將汽車及其車主變成石頭。
但是，它們當中還沒有任何一個變成石頭呢！

title: Taxi
titleTranslate: 福塞特船長認為有哪次旅行是過於危險的嗎？
text:
Does Captain Fawcett think any trip is too dangerous?
Captain Ben Fawcett has bought an unusual taxi and has begun a new service.
The 'taxi' is a small Swiss aeroplane called a 'Pilatus Porter'.
This wonderful plane can carry seven passengers.
The most surprising thing about it, however, is that it can land anywhere: on snow, water, or even on a ploughed field.
Captain Fawcett's first passenger was a doctor who flew from Birmingham to a lonely village in the Welsh mountains.
Since then, Captain Fawcett has flown passengers to many unusual places.
Once he landed on the roof of a block of flats and on another occasion, he landed in a deserted car park.
Captain Fawcett has just refused a strange request from a businessman.
The man wanted to fly to Rockall, a lonely island in the Atlantic Ocean,
but Captain Fawcett did not take him because the trip was too dangerous.
textTranslate:
福塞特船長認為有哪次旅行是過於危險的嗎？
本·福塞特船長買了一輛與眾不同的計程車，並開始提供一項新的服務。
這架“計程車”其實是一架小型瑞士飛機，被稱為“Pilatus Porter”。
這架出色的飛機可以搭載七名乘客。
然而，最令人驚訝的是：它可以在任何地方降落——無論是雪地上、水面上，還是已經翻耕過的田地裡。
福塞特船長的第一位乘客是一位醫生，他乘坐飛機從伯明翰飛往威爾士山區的一個偏遠村莊。
從那以後，福塞特船長已經把乘客送到了許多不尋常的地方。
有一次，他把飛機降落在一棟公寓樓的屋頂上；還有一次，他降落在一個廢棄的停車場裡。
福塞特船長剛剛拒絕了一位商人的奇怪請求。
這個人想要飛往羅卡爾島（Rockall），那是大西洋上一座孤寂的島嶼。
但是福塞特船長沒有帶他去，因為這次旅行太危險了。

title: Football or Polo
titleTranslate: 船上的那個男人怎麼了？
text:
What happened to the man in the boat?
The Wayle is a small river that cuts across the park near my home.
I like sitting by the Wayle on fine afternoons.
It was warm last Sunday, so I went and sat on the river bank as usual.
Some children were playing games on the bank and there were some people rowing on the river.
Suddenly, one of the children kicked a ball very hard and it went towards a passing boat.
Some people on the bank called out to the man in the boat, but he did not hear them.
The ball struck him so hard that he nearly fell into the water.
I turned to look at the children, but there weren't any in sight: they had all run away!
The man laughed when he realized what had happened.
He called out to the children and threw the ball back to the bank.
textTranslate:
船上的那個男人怎麼了？
韋爾河是一條小河，流經我家附近的公園。
我喜歡在天氣好的下午坐在韋爾河畔。
上週日天氣很暖和，所以我像往常一樣去河邊坐著。
有些孩子在河岸上玩遊戲，還有一些人在河上划船。
突然，其中一個孩子用力踢了一個球，球飛向了一艘正在經過的船。
河岸上的一些人向船上的男人喊話，但他沒有聽到。
球重重地打在他身上，差點把他撞進水裡。
我轉身看向那些孩子，但他們已經不見了——他們都跑掉了！
當那個男人意識到發生了什麼時，他笑了起來。
他朝孩子們喊了一聲，然後把球扔回了岸上。

title: Success Story
titleTranslate: 弗蘭克的第一份工作是什麼？
text:
What was Frank's first job?
Yesterday afternoon Frank Hawkins was telling me about
his experiences as a young man.
Before he retired,
Frank was the head of
a very large business company, but as a boy he used to work in a small shop.
It was his job to repair bicycles
and at that time he used to work fourteen hours a day.
He saved money for years and
in 1958 he bought a small workshop of his own.
In his twenties Frank used to make spare parts for aeroplanes.
At that time he had two helpers.
In a few years the small workshop had become a large factory
which employed seven hundred and twenty-eight people.
Frank smiled when he remembered his hard early years and the long road to success.
He was still smiling when the door opened and his wife came in.
She wanted him to repair their grandson's bicycle!
textTranslate:
弗蘭克的第一份工作是什麼？
昨天下午，弗蘭克·霍金斯給我講起了
他年輕時的經歷。
在他退休之前，
弗蘭克是一家
這是一家規模非常大的企業，但小時候他曾在一家小商店裡工作過。
他的工作就是修理腳踏車。
那時，他每天要工作十四個小時。
他多年來一直存錢，
1958年，他買下了屬於自己的一個小作坊。
二十多歲的時候，弗蘭克經常為飛機制造零部件。
當時他有兩個助手。
幾年後，那個小作坊已經發展成了一家大工廠。
這家工廠僱用了七百二十八名員工。
弗蘭克想起自己早年艱難的歲月以及通往成功的漫長道路時，露出了微笑。
門開啟時，他仍在微笑，他的妻子走了進來。
她讓他去修理孫子的腳踏車！

title: Shopping Made Easy
titleTranslate: 誰是小偷？
text:
Who was the thief?
People are not so honest as
they once were.
The temptation to steal is greater than
ever before--especially in large shops.
A detective recently watched a well-dressed
woman who always went into a large store
on Monday mornings.
One Monday,
there were fewer people in the shop
than usual when the woman came in,
so it was easier for the detective to watch her.
The woman first bought a few small articles.
After a little time,
she chose one of the most expensive dresses
in the shop and handed it to an assistant
who wrapped it up for her as quickly as possible.
Then the woman simply took the parcel
and walked out of the shop without paying.
When she was arrested,
the detective found out that
the shop assistant was her daughter.
The girl 'gave' her mother
a free dress once a week!
textTranslate:
誰是小偷？
人們並沒有那麼誠實……
他們曾經那麼誠實。
偷竊的誘惑力比……（原句後半部分缺失，無法完整翻譯）
前所未有——尤其是在大型商店裡。
最近，一名偵探觀察了一名穿著考究的人……
她總是去一家大型商店
每週一早上。
一個星期一的早晨，
商店裡的人變少了。
當那位女士走進來時，商店裡的人比平時少。
因此，偵探更容易監視她了。
這位女士首先購買了幾件小商品。
過了一會兒，
她選了一條價格最貴的裙子。
然後把它交給一個店員
店員儘快為她包好了衣服。
然後，那個女人直接拿走了那個包裹。
然後沒付錢就走出了商店。
當她被逮捕時，
偵探發現……
那位店員其實是她的女兒。
那個女孩每週'給'她母親
每週可以免費獲得一件連衣裙！

title: Out of the Darkness
titleTranslate: 為什麼那個女孩要住院呢？
text:
Why was the girl in hospital?
Nearly a week passed before
the girl was able to explain
what had happened to her.
One afternoon she set out
from the coast in a small boat
and was caught in a storm.
Towards evening,
the boat struck a rock and
the girl jumped into the sea.
Then she swam to the shore
after spending the whole night in the water.
During that time she covered
a distance of eight miles.
Early next morning,
she saw a light ahead.
She knew she was near the shore
because the light was high up on the cliffs.
On arriving at the shore,
the girl struggled up the cliff towards
the light she had seen.
That was all she remembered.
When she woke up a day later,
she found herself in hospital.
textTranslate:
為什麼那個女孩要住院呢？
差不多過了一個星期，那個女孩才
能夠解釋清楚
她身上到底發生了什麼事。
一天下午，她
從海岸乘坐一艘小船出發。
結果被一場暴風雨困住了。
到了傍晚時分，
船撞上了一塊岩石。
那個女孩跳進了海里。
然後她游到了岸邊。
在水中度過了一整夜之後，
遊了
八英里的距離。
第二天清晨，
她看到前方有一道光。
她知道自己已經接近海岸了。
因為燈光位於懸崖的高處。
到達岸邊後，
那個女孩費力地爬上懸崖，朝著
她看到的那道光爬去。
那就是她所記得的全部內容了。
一天後醒來時，
她被送進了醫院。

title: Quick Work
titleTranslate: 警察花了多長時間才找到他的腳踏車？
text:
How long had the police taken to find his bicycle?
Dan Robinson has been worried all week.
Last Tuesday he received a letter
from the local police.
In the letter he was asked to call at the station.
Dan wondered why
he was wanted by the police,
but he went to the station yesterday
and now he is not worried anymore.
At the station,
he was told by a smiling policeman
that his bicycle had been found.
Five days ago, the policeman told him,
the bicycle was picked up in a small village
four hundred miles away.
It is now being sent to his home by train.
Dan was most surprised
when he heard the news.
He was amused too,
because he never expected
the bicycle to be found.
It was stolen twenty years ago
when Dan was a boy of fifteen!
textTranslate:
警察花了多長時間才找到他的腳踏車？
丹·羅賓遜整個星期都一直很擔心。
上週二，他收到了一封信。
來自當地警方。
信中要求他去一趟警察局。
丹想知道為什麼。
警察找他有什麼事。
但他昨天去車站了。
現在他不再擔心了。
在車站裡，
一位面帶微笑的警察告訴他
他的腳踏車已經被找到了。
五天前，警察對他說：
這輛腳踏車是在一個小村莊裡被發現的。
四百英里之外。
現在正透過火車運往他家。
丹感到非常驚訝。
當他聽到這個訊息時……
他也很覺得有趣。
因為他從沒想過
這輛腳踏車還能被找到。
它是在二十年前被偷走的。
那是丹十五歲的時候！

title: Stop Thief
titleTranslate: 羅伊是如何阻止那些小偷的？
text:
How did Roy stop the thieves?
Roy Trenton used to drive a taxi.
A short while ago, however,
he became a bus driver
and he has not regretted it.
He is finding his new work far more exciting.
When he was driving
along Catford Street recently,
he saw two thieves rush out of a shop
and run towards a waiting car.
One of them was carrying a bag full of money.
Roy acted quickly and drove the bus
straight at the thieves.
The one with the money got such a fright
that he dropped the bag.
As the thieves were trying to get away in their car,
Roy drove his bus into the back of it.
While the battered car was moving away,
Roy stopped his bus and telephoned the police.
The thieves' car was badly damaged and easy to recognize.
Shortly afterwards,
the police stopped the car and both men were arrested.
textTranslate:
羅伊是如何阻止那些小偷的？
羅伊·特倫頓以前開過計程車。
然而，就在不久前……
他成了一名公交車司機。
他從未為此感到後悔。
他發現自己的新工作要有趣得多。
當他開車的時候
最近，在卡特福德街（Catford Street）上……
他看到兩個小偷從商店裡衝了出來。
然後跑向一輛正在等待的車輛。
其中一個人帶著一個裝滿錢的袋子。
羅伊反應迅速，立刻駕駛著公交車
直接衝向那些小偷。
那個拿錢的人嚇了一大跳
以至於他掉了那個袋子
當小偷們試圖開車逃跑時，
羅伊駕駛他的公交車撞上了那輛車的後部。
當那輛被撞壞的汽車開走時
羅伊停下了他的公交車，然後撥打了報警電話。
小偷們的汽車損壞嚴重，因此很容易被辨認出來。
不久之後，
警察攔下了那輛車，兩名男子都被逮捕了。

title: Across the Channel
titleTranslate: 黛比打算做什麼呢？
text:
What is Debbie going to try to do?
Debbie Hart is going to swim
across the English Channel tomorrow.
She is going to set out from the French coast
at five o'clock in the morning.
Debbie is only eleven years old
and she hopes to set up a new world record.
She is a strong swimmer and many people feel
that she is sure to succeed.
Debbie's father will set out with her
in a small boat.
Mr. Hart has trained his daughter for years.
Tomorrow he will be watching her anxiously
as she swims the long distance to England.
Debbie intends to take short rests every two hours.
She will have something to drink
but she will not eat any solid food.
Most of Debbie's school friends will be
waiting for her on the English coast.
Among them will be Debbie's mother,
who swam the Channel herself
when she was a girl.
textTranslate:
黛比打算做什麼呢？
黛比·哈特將要游泳
橫渡英吉利海峽。
她將從法國海岸出發。
早上五點。
黛比只有十一歲。
她希望能夠創造一項新的世界紀錄。
她是個游泳健將，很多人都認為
她一定會成功。
黛比的父親會和她一起出發。
乘坐一艘小船。
哈特先生多年來一直在訓練他的女兒。
明天，他會焦急地注視著她。
當她遊向遙遠的英格蘭時。
黛比打算每兩小時休息一會兒。
她會喝點東西
但她不吃任何固體食物。
黛比的大多數同學都會
在英格蘭的海岸邊等待著她。
其中會包括黛比的母親。
她自己也曾經遊過英吉利海峽
當她還是個女孩的時候。

title: The Olympic Games
titleTranslate: 這個國家上一次舉辦奧運會是什麼時候？
text:
When was the last time this country
hosted the Olympic Games?
The Olympic Games will be held
in our country in four years' time.
As a great many people will be
visiting the country,
the government will be building new hotels,
an immense stadium,
and a new Olympic-standard swimming pool.
They will also be building new roads
and a special railway line.
The Games will be held just outside the capital
and the whole area will be called 'Olympic City'.
Workers will have completed the new roads
by the end of this year.
By the end of next year,
they will have finished work on the new stadium.
The fantastic modern buildings have been designed
by Kurt Gunter.
Everybody will be watching anxiously
as the new buildings go up.
We are all very excited and are looking forward to
the Olympic Games because
they have never been held before in this country.
textTranslate:
這個國家上一次舉辦奧運會是什麼時候？
舉辦奧運會？
奧運會將在我們國家舉行
由於將有大量遊客來訪
政府將建造新酒店
訪問這個國家
政府將會建造新的酒店。
一個巨大的體育場。
以及一個符合奧運標準的游泳池。
他們還將修建新的道路。
以及一條專門的鐵路線。
比賽將在首都郊外舉行。
整個區域將被命名為“奧林匹克城”。
工人們將在今年年底前完成新道路建設
到今年年底為止。
到明年年底為止，
他們將在明年年底前完成新體育場的建設
這些宏偉的現代建築由庫爾特·岡特設計
設計者是庫爾特·岡特
大家都會急切地關注著
新建築的拔地而起
我們都非常興奮，都在期待著……
因為奧運會……
這個國家從未舉辦過奧運會

title: Everything Except the Weather
titleTranslate: 為什麼哈里森這麼快就賣掉了他的房子？
text:
Why did Harrison sell his house so quickly?
My old friend, Harrison, had lived in
the Mediterranean for many years
before he returned to England.
He had often dreamed of retiring in England
and had planned to settle down in the country.
He had no sooner returned than he bought
a house and went to live there.
Almost immediately he began to complain
about the weather,
for even though it was still summer,
it rained continually and it was often bitterly cold.
After so many years of sunshine,
Harrison got a shock.
He acted as if he had never lived in England before.
In the end, it was more than he could bear.
He had hardly had time to settle down
when he sold the house and left the country.
The dream he had had for so many years ended there.
Harrison had thought of everything except the weather.
textTranslate:
為什麼哈里森這麼快就賣掉了他的房子？
我的老朋友哈里森曾經住在這裡。
在地中海地區生活了許多年
在他回到英格蘭之前。
他經常夢想著在英格蘭退休。
並且計劃在鄉下安頓下來
他一回來就買了
一棟房子並搬去那裡住
幾乎立刻，他就開始抱怨起來。
抱怨天氣
因為儘管還是夏天
雨一直下個不停，天氣也常常非常寒冷。
在享受了多年陽光之後
哈里森感到非常震驚。
他的舉止就好像他以前從未在英國生活過一樣。
最終，這超出了他的承受能力。
他幾乎還沒來得及安頓下來呢。
就賣掉了房子離開了這個國家
他懷揣多年的夢想就此破滅
哈里森考慮到了所有可能的因素，唯獨沒有考慮到天氣。

title: Am I All Right
titleTranslate: 為什麼吉爾伯特先生給米林頓醫生打了電話？
text:
Why did Mr. Gilbert telephone Dr. Millington?
While John Gilbert was in hospital,
he asked his doctor to tell him
whether his operation had been successful,
but the doctor refused to do so.
The following day,
the patient asked for a bedside telephone.
When he was alone,
he telephoned the hospital exchange
and asked for Doctor Millington.
When the doctor answered the phone,
Mr. Gilbert said he was inquiring about a certain patient,
a Mr. John Gilbert.
He asked if Mr. Gilbert's operation had been successful
and the doctor told him that it had been.
He then asked when Mr. Gilbert would be allowed to
go home and the doctor told him
that he would have to stay
in hosptial for another two weeks.
Then Dr. Millington asked the caller
if he was a relative of the patient.
'No,' the patient answered,
'I am Mr. John Gilbert.'
textTranslate:
為什麼吉爾伯特先生給米林頓醫生打了電話？
在約翰·吉爾伯特住院期間，
他請求醫生告訴他
他的手術是否成功。
但是醫生拒絕這樣做。
第二天，
患者要求配備一臺床邊電話。
當他獨自一人時，
他撥打了醫院的電話總機。
然後他要求找米林頓醫生接電話。
當醫生接聽電話時，
吉爾伯特先生說，他正在詢問關於某位病人的情況。
一位名叫約翰·吉爾伯特的先生。
他詢問吉爾伯特先生的手術是否成功。
醫生告訴他，手術是成功的。
他接著詢問吉爾伯特先生什麼時候可以
回家，醫生告訴他
他必須
他還需要在醫院住院兩週。
然後，米林頓博士詢問了來電者。
他是不是病人的親屬。
“不，”病人回答道。
我是約翰·吉爾伯特先生。

title: Food and Talk
titleTranslate: 蘭博爾德夫人在晚餐時是個不錯的交談夥伴嗎？
text:
Was Mrs. Rumbold a good companion at dinner?
Last week at a dinner party,
the hostess asked me to sit next to Mrs. Rumbold.
Mrs. Rumbold was a large,
unsmiling lady in a tight black dress.
She did not even look up when I took my seat beside her.
Her eyes were fixed on her plate and in a short time,
she was busy eating.
I tried to make conversation.
'A new play is coming to "The Globe" soon,'
I said. 'Will you be seeing it?'
'No,' she answered.
'Will you be spending your holidays abroad this year?' I asked.
'No,' she answered.
'Will you be staying in England?' I asked.
'No,' she answered.
In despair, I asked her whether she was enjoying her dinner.
'Young man,' she answered,
'if you ate more and talked less, we would both enjoy our dinner!'
textTranslate:
蘭博爾德夫人在晚餐時是個不錯的交談夥伴嗎？
上週在一個晚宴上，
女主人讓我坐在蘭博爾德夫人旁邊。
蘭博爾德太太身材高大，
那位面無笑容的女士穿著一件緊身的黑色連衣裙。
當我坐在她旁邊時，她甚至都沒有抬頭看我一眼。
她的目光緊緊盯著自己的盤子，不一會兒……
她當時正忙著吃飯。
我試著和她交談。
一部新劇很快將在“The Globe”劇院上演。
我說：“您會去看嗎？”
“不，”她回答道。
“今年你打算在國外度過假期嗎？”我問道。
“不，”她回答。
“那你會留在英國嗎？”我又問。
“不，”她回答。
我有些絕望，便問她晚餐吃得怎麼樣。
“年輕人，”她說道，
“如果你多吃點、少說點話，我們倆的晚餐都會更愉快！”

title: Do You Call That a Hat
titleTranslate: 你覺得這位作家喜歡哪種型別的購物呢？
text:
What kind of shopping does the writer enjoy, do you think?
'Do you call that a hat?' I said to my wife.
'You needn't be so rude about it,'
my wife answered as she looked at herself in the mirror.
I sat down on one of those modern chairs with holes in it and waited.
We had been in the hat shop for half an hour and my wife was still in front of the mirror.
'We mustn't buy things we don't need,' I remarked suddenly.
I regretted saying it almost at once.
'You needn't have said that,' my wife answered.
'I needn't remind you of that terrible tie you bought yesterday.'
'I find it beautiful,' I said. 'A man can never have too many ties.'
'And a woman can't have too many hats,' she answered.
Ten minutes later we walked out of the shop together.
My wife was wearing a hat that looked like a lighthouse!
textTranslate:
你覺得這位作家喜歡哪種型別的購物呢？
“你那叫帽子嗎？”我對我的妻子說道。
“你沒必要這麼無禮啊。”
我妻子一邊看著鏡子中的自己，一邊回答了我。
我坐在一把帶洞的現代椅子上等著。
我們在那家帽子店已經待了半個小時了，而我妻子仍然站在鏡子前。
“我們不應該購買那些我們不需要的東西，”我突然說道。
我幾乎立刻就後悔說了那句話。
“你其實沒必要那麼說，”我的妻子回答道。
“我其實沒必要再提醒你昨天你買的那條糟糕的領帶了。”
“我覺得它很漂亮，”我說，“一個男人有多少領帶也不嫌多。”
“而女人的帽子也是有多少都不嫌多，”她回答道。
十分鐘後，我們一起走出了那家商店。
我妻子戴著一頂看起來像燈塔的帽子！

title: Not Very Musical
titleTranslate: 當那個耍蛇的人開始演奏爵士樂時，發生了什麼？
text:
What happened when the snake charmer began to play jazz?
As we had had a long walk through one of the markets of old Delhi,
we stopped at a square to have a rest.
After a time, we noticed a snake charmer with
two large baskets at the other side of the square,
so we went to have a look at him.
As soon as he saw us,
he picked up a long pipe which was covered with coins and opened one of the baskets.
When he began to play a tune,
we had our first glimpse of the snake.
It rose out of the basket and began to follow the movements of the pipe.
We were very much surprised when the snake charmer
suddenly began to play jazz and modern pop songs.
The snake, however, continued to 'dance' slowly.
It obviously could not tell the difference between Indian music and jazz!
textTranslate:
當那個耍蛇的人開始演奏爵士樂時，發生了什麼？
我們沿著舊德里的一個市場走了很長一段路後，
我們在一個廣場上停下來休息。
過了一段時間，我們注意到有一個耍蛇的人……
在廣場的另一邊，帶著兩個大籃子。
於是我們便走過去看看他。
他一看到我們，
他拿起一根綴滿硬幣的長笛，然後開啟了其中一個籃子。
當他開始演奏一首曲子時，
我們第一次看到了那條蛇。
它從籃子裡探出身來，開始隨著笛子的擺動而舞動。
當那個耍蛇的人突然開始演奏爵士樂和現代流行歌曲時，我們非常驚訝。
他突然開始演奏爵士樂和現代流行歌曲。
然而，那條蛇仍然繼續緩慢地“跳舞”。
顯然，它根本分不清印度音樂和爵士樂的區別！

title: Over the South Pole
titleTranslate: 這架飛機是如何成功飛越那些山脈的呢？
text:
How was the plane able to clear the mountains?
In 1929, three years after his flight over the North Pole,
the American explorer, R.E. Byrd,
successfully flew over the South Pole for the first time.
Though, at first, Byrd and his men were able to take a
great many photographs of the mountains that lay below,
they soon ran into serious trouble.
At one point, it seemed certain that their plane would crash.
It could only get over the mountains if it rose to 10, 000 feet.
Byrd at once ordered his men to throw out two heavy food sacks.
The plane was then able to rise and it cleared the mountains by 400 feet.
Byrd now knew that he would be able to reach the South Pole which was 300 miles away,
for there were no more mountains in sight.
The aircraft was able to fly over the endless white plains without difficulty.
textTranslate:
這架飛機是如何成功飛越那些山脈的呢？
1929年，在他飛越北極三年後……
美國探險家 R.E. 比爾德（R.E. Byrd）
它首次成功飛越了南極。
不過，起初伯德和他的手下們確實拍下了大量的照片，
大量的照片記錄了下方那些山脈的景象。
他們很快就遇到了嚴重的麻煩。
有那麼一刻，人們確信他們的飛機將會墜毀。
只有當它上升到 10,000 英尺的高度時，才能翻越那些山脈。
伯德立刻命令他的手下扔掉兩個沉重的食物袋。
飛機於是得以爬升，並以400英尺的高度飛越了山脈。
伯德現在知道，他能夠到達300英里外的南極點了，
因為眼前已經沒有更多的山脈了。
飛機得以毫不費力地飛越那片無盡的白色平原。

title: Through the Forest
titleTranslate: 斯特林夫人是怎麼拿回她的包的？
text:
How did Mrs. Sterling get her bag back?
Mrs. Anne Sterling did not think of the risk
she was taking when she ran through a forest after two men.
They had rushed up to her while she was having a picnic at the edge of a forest
with her children and tried to steal her handbag.
In the struggle, the strap broke and,
with the bag in their possession,
both men started running through the trees.
Mrs. Sterling got so angry that she ran after them.
She was soon out of breath,
but she continued to run.
When she caught up with them,
she saw that they had sat down and were going through the contents of the bag,
so she ran straight at them.
The men got such a fright that they dropped the bag and ran away.
'The strap needs mending,' said Mrs. Sterling later, 'but they did not steal anything.'
textTranslate:
斯特林夫人是怎麼拿回她的包的？
安妮·斯特林夫人並沒有考慮到其中的風險。
她追趕那兩個男人時，穿過了一片森林。
當她在森林邊緣野餐時，他們立刻跑向了她。
她帶著孩子們野餐時，他們衝上來試圖偷她的手提包。
在掙扎的過程中，那根帶子斷了。
拿著那個包，
兩名男子開始在樹林中奔跑。
斯特林太太氣得衝了上去追趕他們。
她很快就氣喘吁吁了。
但她還是繼續奔跑著。
當她追上他們時，
她看到他們已經坐下來，正在翻看包裡的東西。
於是她直接朝他們衝了過去。
那兩個男人嚇了一大跳，扔下包就跑了。
“這條帶子需要修理了，”斯特林夫人後來說道，“但他們什麼也沒偷。”

title: A Clear Conscience
titleTranslate: 薩姆是怎麼把錢拿回來的？
text:
How did Sam get his money back?
The whole village soon learnt that a large sum of money had been lost.
Sam Benton, the local butcher,
had lost his wallet while taking his savings to the post office.
Sam was sure that the wallet must have been found by one of the villagers,
but it was not returned to him.
Three months passed, and then one morning, Sam found his wallet outside his front door.
It had been wrapped up in newspaper and it contained half the money he had lost,
together with a note which said:
'A thief, yes, but only 50 per cent a thief!'
Two months later,
some more money was sent to Sam with another note:
'Only 25 per cent a thief now!'
In time, all Sam's money was paid back in this way.
The last note said: 'I am 100 per cent honest now!'
textTranslate:
薩姆是怎麼把錢拿回來的？
整個村莊很快都知道有一大筆錢丟失了。
山姆·本頓，當地的肉鋪老闆……
他在去郵局存錢的時候丟失了錢包。
薩姆確信那個錢包一定是被某個村民找到的。
但是，那個東西並沒有被歸還給他。
三個月過去了，一天早晨，山姆發現他的錢包放在自家前門外面。
它被用報紙包了起來，裡面裝著他丟失的錢的一半。
還附有一張紙條，上面寫著：
“他確實是個小偷，但只能算是個‘50%的小偷’罷了！”
兩個月後，
又有一些錢被寄給了薩姆，並附上了一張便條。
“現在只有25%的人是小偷了！”
隨著時間的推移，山姆的所有錢都透過這種方式被還清了。
最後一張紙條上寫著：“現在我100%是誠實的了！”

title: Expensive and Uncomfortable
titleTranslate: 這個故事裡的男人做了什麼？
text:
What did the man in this story do?
When a plane from London arrived at Sydney airport,
workers began to unload a number of wooden boxes which contained clothing.
No one could account for the fact that one of the boxes was extremely heavy.
It suddenly occurred to one of the workers to open up the box.
He was astonished at what he found.
A man was lying in the box on top of a pile of woollen goods.
He was so surprised at being discovered that he did not even try to run away.
After he was arrested,
the man admitted hiding in the box before the plane left London.
He had had a long and uncomfortable trip,
for he had been confined to the wooden box for over eighteen hours.
The man was ordered to pay ￡3, 500 for the cost of the trip.
The normal price of a ticket is ￡2, 000!
textTranslate:
這個故事裡的男人做了什麼？
當一架從倫敦起飛的飛機抵達悉尼機場時，
工人們開始卸下許多裝有衣物的木箱。
沒有人能夠解釋為什麼其中一個箱子會異常地重。
突然，其中一個工人想到應該開啟那個箱子。
他對自己的發現感到非常驚訝。
一個男人躺在箱子裡，躺在一堆羊毛製品的上面。
他發現自己被發現了，驚訝得連逃跑的念頭都沒有。
在他被逮捕之後，
那名男子承認在飛機離開倫敦之前躲在了那個箱子裡。
他經歷了一段漫長而令人不適的旅程。
因為他已經被關在那個木箱裡超過十八個小時了。
這名男子被責令支付3,500英鎊的旅費。
而一張普通機票的價格才2,000英鎊！

title: A Thirsty Ghost
titleTranslate: 有什麼證據表明有鬼存在呢？
text:
What evidence is there of a ghost?
A public house which was recently bought by Mr.Ian Thompson is up for sale.
Mr.Thompson is going to sell it because it is haunted.
He told me that he could not go to sleep one night
because he heard a strange noise coming from the bar.
The next morning,
he found that the doors had been blocked by chairs and the furniture had been moved.
Though Mr.Thompson had turned the lights off before he went to bed,
they were on in the morning.
He also said that he had found five empty whisky bottles
which the ghost must have drunk the night before.
When I suggested that some villagers must have come
in for a free drink, Mr.Thompson shook his head.
The villagers have told him that
they will not accept the pub even if he gives it away.
textTranslate:
有什麼證據表明有鬼存在呢？
一家最近被伊恩·湯普森先生購買的酒吧正在出售中。
湯普森先生打算賣掉它，因為那裡鬧鬼。
他告訴我，有一天晚上他無法入睡
因為他聽到從酒吧裡傳來一陣奇怪的聲音。
第二天早上，
他發現門被椅子堵住了，傢俱也被挪動了。
儘管湯普森先生在睡覺前已經關掉了燈，
早上燈卻亮著。
他還說，他發現了五個空威士忌酒瓶。
肯定是鬼前一天晚上喝掉的。
當我提出“肯定有一些村民來過這裡”這個想法時……
湯普森先生搖了搖頭。
村民們告訴他：
“就算他把酒吧免費送出去，他們也不會接受的。”

title: Did You Want to Tell Me Something
titleTranslate: 為什麼作者會變得非常擔心呢？
text:
Why did the writer become very worried?
Dentists always ask questions when it is impossible for you to answer.
My dentist had just pulled out one of my teeth and had told me to rest for a while.
I tried to say something,
but my mouth was full of cotton wool.
He knew I collected match boxes and asked me whether my collection was growing.
He then asked me how my brother was and whether I liked my new job in London.
In answer to these questions I either nodded or made strange noises.
Meanwhile, my tongue was busy searching out the hole where the tooth had been.
I suddenly felt very worried, but could not say anything.
When the dentist at last removed the cotton wool from my mouth,
I was able to tell him that he had pulled out the wrong tooth.
textTranslate:
為什麼作者會變得非常擔心呢？
牙醫總是在你無法回答問題的時候提問。
我的牙醫剛剛拔掉了我的一顆牙齒，然後讓我休息一會兒。
我試圖說些什麼，
但我的嘴裡塞滿了棉花。
他知道我收集火柴盒，便問我收藏的火柴盒是不是越來越多了。
接著他又問我弟弟的情況，以及我是否喜歡在倫敦的新工作。
對於這些問題，我只能點頭或者發出一些奇怪的聲音作為回答。
與此同時，我的舌頭不停地探尋著那顆牙齒被拔掉後留下的窟窿。
我突然感到非常擔心，但卻什麼也說不出來。
直到牙醫終於把嘴裡的棉花取出來，
我才告訴他，他拔錯了牙齒。

title: The End of a Dream
titleTranslate: 這個夢是怎麼結束的？
text:
How did the dream end?
Tired of sleeping on the floor,
a young man in Teheran saved up for years to buy a real bed.
For the first time in his life,
he became the proud owner of a bed which had springs and a mattress.
Because the weather was very hot,
he carried the bed on to the roof of his house.
He slept very well for the first two nights,
but on the third night, a storm blew up.
A gust of wind swept the bed off the roof and sent it crashing into the courtyard below.
The young man did not wake up
until the bed had struck the ground.
Although the bed was smashed to pieces,
the man was miraculously unhurt.
When he woke up, he was still on the mattress.
Glancing at the bits of wood and metal that lay around him,
the man sadly picked up the mattress and carried it into his house.
After he had put it on the floor,
he promptly went to sleep again.
textTranslate:
這個夢是怎麼結束的？
受夠了睡在地板上……
在德黑蘭，一個年輕人為了買一張真正的床，攢了多年的錢。
他生平第一次……
他驕傲地擁有了一張配有彈簧和床墊的床。
因為天氣非常炎熱，
他把那張床搬到了自己房子的屋頂上。
他在頭兩個晚上睡得非常好。
但是到了第三天晚上，一場暴風雨突然降臨了。
一陣大風把床從屋頂上吹了下去，哐噹一聲掉進了下面的院子裡。
那個年輕人沒有醒來。
直到床砸到地上他才醒來。
儘管床被砸得粉碎，
那個人卻奇蹟般地毫髮無傷。
當他醒來時，發現自己仍然躺在床墊上。
他瞥了一眼散落在周圍的木頭和金屬碎片，
他傷心地撿起床墊，把它搬回了屋裡。
把床墊放在地板上後，
他立刻又睡著了。

title: Taken for a Ride
titleTranslate: 為什麼作者沒有在伍德福德格林（Woodford Green）下車呢？
text:
Why did the writer not get off the bus at Woodford Green?
I love travelling in the country,
but I don't like losing my way.
I went on an excursion recently,
but my trip took me longer than I expected.
'I'm going to Woodford Green,'
I said to the conductor as I got on the bus,
'but I don't know where it is.'
'I'll tell you where to get off.' answered the conductor.
I sat in the front of the bus to get a good view of the countryside.
After some time, the bus stopped.
Looking round, I realized with a shock that I was the only passenger left on the bus.
'You'll have to get off here,'
the conductor said. 'This is as far as we go.'
'Is this Woodford Green?' I asked.
'Oh dear,' said the conductor suddenly.
'I forgot to put you off.'
'It doesn't matter,' I said. 'I'll get off here.'
'We're going back now,' said the conductor.
'Well, in that case,
I prefer to stay on the bus,' I answered.
textTranslate:
為什麼作者沒有在伍德福德格林（Woodford Green）下車呢？
我喜歡在鄉村旅行。
但我不喜歡迷路。
我最近去參加了一次短途旅行。
但是我的旅行花費的時間比我預期的要長。
“我要去伍德福德格林。”
當我登上公交車時，我對售票員說：
“但我不知道它在哪裡。”
“我會告訴您在哪下車。”售票員回答道。
我坐在公交車的前排，以便能更好地欣賞鄉村的景色。
過了一段時間，公交車停了下來。
環顧四周後，我震驚地發現自己是公交車上唯一剩下的乘客。
“你得從這裡下去。”
售票員說：“我們就到這兒了。”
“這是伍德福德格林（Woodford Green）嗎？”我問道。
“哦，天哪，”售票員突然說道。
“我忘了讓您下車。”
“沒關係，”我說，“我就在這兒下車。”
“我們現在要返回了，”售票員說。
“嗯，既然是這樣的話，
“我更願意待在公交車上，”我回答道。

title: Reward for Virtue
titleTranslate: 為什麼休的節食計劃沒有效果呢？
text:
Why did Hugh's diet not work?
My friend, Hugh, has always been fat,
but things got so bad recently that he decided to go on a diet.
He began his diet a week ago.
First of all, he wrote out a long list of all the foods which were forbidden.
The list included most of the things Hugh loves:
butter, potatoes, rice, beer, milk, chocolate, and sweets.
Yesterday I paid him a visit.
I rang the bell and was not surprised to see that Hugh was still as fat as ever.
He led me into his room and hurriedly hid a large parcel under his desk.
It was obvious that he was very embarrassed.
When I asked him what he was doing,
he smiled guiltily and then put the parcel on the desk.
He explained that his diet was so strict that he had to reward himself occasionally.
Then he showed me the contents of the parcel.
It contained five large bars of chocolate and three bags of sweets!
textTranslate:
為什麼休的節食計劃沒有效果呢？
我的朋友休一直都很胖。
但最近情況變得非常糟糕，以至於他決定開始節食。
他一週前開始節食了。
首先，他列出了一份長長的清單，上面列出了所有被禁止食用的食物。
這份清單上列出了休最喜歡的大多數東西。
黃油、土豆、米飯、啤酒、牛奶、巧克力和糖果。
昨天我去拜訪了他。
我按響了門鈴，看到休還是和以前一樣胖，並沒有感到驚訝。
他把我帶進了他的房間，然後匆忙地把一個大包裹藏在了桌子下面。
很明顯，他感到非常尷尬。
當我問他正在做什麼時，
他內疚地笑了笑，然後把包裹放在了桌子上。
他解釋說，他的飲食控制非常嚴格，因此偶爾需要獎勵自己一下。
然後他向我展示了包裹裡的物品。
裡面是五塊大巧克力和三袋糖果！

title: A Pretty Carpet
titleTranslate: 作者的“地毯”（這裡可能指作者的辦公環境或工作場所）是由什麼材料製成的呢？
text:
What is the writer's carpet made of?
We have just moved into a new house and I have been working hard all morning.
I have been trying to get my new room in order.
This has not been easy because I own over a thousand books.
To make matters worse,
the room is rather small,
so I have temporarily put my books on the floor.
At the moment, they cover every inch of floor space
and I actually have to walk on them to get in or out of the room.
A short while ago, my sister helped me to carry one of my old bookcases up the stairs.
She went into my room and got a big surprise when she saw all those books on the floor.
'This is the prettiest carpet I have ever seen,' she said.
She gazed at it for some time then added,
'You don't need bookcases at all.
You can sit here in your spare time and read the carpet!'
textTranslate:
作者的“地毯”（這裡可能指作者的辦公環境或工作場所）是由什麼材料製成的呢？
我們剛搬進了一棟新房子，我一整個早上都在努力工作。
我一直在努力把我的新房間整理得井井有條。
這並不容易，因為我有超過一千本書。
更糟糕的是……
這個房間相當小。
所以我暫時把我的書放在了地板上。
目前，它們已經覆蓋了每一寸的地面空間。
實際上，我必須踩在這些東西上才能進入或離開房間。
不久前，我的姐姐幫我把其中一箇舊書櫃搬上了樓梯。
她走進我的房間，看到地板上堆滿了書，頓時感到非常驚訝。
“這是我見過最漂亮的地毯了，”她說。
她凝視著它一會兒，然後補充道：
“你根本不需要書架。
你可以在空閒時間坐在這裡，直接在地毯上閱讀！”

title: Hot Snake
titleTranslate: 是什麼導致了這場火災？
text:
What caused the fire?
At last firemen have put out a big forest fire in California.
Since then, they have been trying to find out how the fire began.
Forest fires are often caused by broken glass or by cigarette ends which people carelessly throw away.
Yesterday the firemen examined the ground carefully,
but were not able to find any broken glass.
They were also quite sure that a cigarette end did not start the fire.
This morning, however, a firemen accidentally discovered the cause.
He noticed the remains of a snake which was wound round the electric wires of a 16, 000-volt power line.
In this way, he was able to solve the mystery.
The explanation was simple but very unusual.
A bird had snatched up the snake from the ground and then dropped it on to the wires.
The snake then wound itself round the wires.
When it did so, it sent sparks down to the ground and these immediately started a fire.
textTranslate:
是什麼導致了這場火災？
消防員們終於撲滅了加利福尼亞州的那場大規模森林火災。
從那以後，他們一直在試圖查明火災的起因。
森林火災通常是由碎玻璃或人們隨意丟棄的菸頭引起的。
昨天，消防員們仔細地檢查了那片地面。
但他們沒有發現任何碎玻璃。
他們也非常確定，火災並不是由菸頭引起的。
然而，今天早上，一名消防員意外地發現了火災的原因。
他發現了一條蛇的殘骸，那條蛇纏繞在一條16,000伏高壓電線上。
透過這種方式，他終於解開了這個謎團。
這個解釋很簡單，但卻非常不尋常。
一隻鳥從地上抓起了那條蛇，然後把它扔到了電線上。
然後，那條蛇纏住了那些電線。
當它這樣做時，火花濺到了地上，立刻引發了火災。

title: Sticky Fingers
titleTranslate: 作者遇到了哪兩種打擾？
text:
What two interruptions did the writer have?
After breakfast, I sent the children to school and then I went to the shops.
It was still early when I returned home.
The children were at school, my husband was at work and the house was quiet.
So I decided to make some meat pies.
In a short time I was busy mixing butter
and flour and my hands were soon covered with sticky pastry.
At exactly that moment, the telephone rang.
Nothing could have been more annoying.
I picked up the receiver between two sticky fingers and was dismayed when I recognized the voice of Helen Bates.
It took me ten minutes to persuade her to ring back later. At last I hung up the receiver.
What a mess! There was pastry on my fingers, on the telephone, and on the doorknobs.
I had no sooner got back to the kitchen than the doorbell rang loud enough to wake the dead.
This time it was the postman and he wanted me to sign for a registered letter!
textTranslate:
作者遇到了哪兩種打擾？
早餐後，我送孩子們去了學校，然後去了商店。
當我回到家時，時間還早。
孩子們都在學校，我丈夫也在上班，家裡一片安靜。
所以我決定做一些肉餡派。
沒過多久，我就忙著攪拌黃油了。
和麵粉，很快我的雙手就沾滿了粘糊糊的油酥麵糰。
就在那一刻，電話響了。
沒有什麼比這更讓人惱火了。
我用兩個沾滿油酥的手指拿起聽筒，當聽出是海倫·貝茨的聲音時，我感到非常沮喪。
我花了十分鐘的時間才說服她稍後回電話。最後，我終於結束通話了電話。
真是一團糟！我的手指上、電話上，還有門把手上都沾滿了油酥麵糰。
我剛回到廚房，門鈴就響得震天響，彷彿連死人都會被吵醒。
這次來的是郵遞員，他讓我簽收一封掛號信！

title: Not a Gold Mine
titleTranslate: 團隊發現了什麼？
text:
What did the team find?
Dreams of finding lost treasure almost came true recently.
A new machine called 'The Revealer' has been invented
and it has been used to detect gold which has been buried in the ground.
The machine was used in a cave near the seashore where--it is said--pirates used to hide gold.
The pirates would often bury gold in the cave and then fail to collect it.
Armed with the new machine, a search party went into the cave hoping to find buried treasure.
The leader of the party was examining the soil near the entrance to the cave when the machine showed that there was gold under the ground.
Very excited, the party dug a hole two feet deep.
They finally found a small gold coin which was almost worthless.
The party then searched the whole cave thoroughly but did not find anything except an empty tin trunk.
In spite of this, many people are confident that 'The Revealer' may reveal something of value fairly soon.
textTranslate:
團隊發現了什麼？
最近，找到失寶藏的夢想幾乎成真了。
一種名為“The Revealer”的新機器已經被髮明出來了。
它已被用於探測埋藏在地下的黃金。
這臺機器被用於海邊附近的一個洞穴中；據說，海盜們過去常常在那裡藏匿黃金。
海盜們經常把黃金埋藏在洞穴裡，但後來卻沒能把那些黃金取出來。
帶著這臺新機器，一支搜尋隊進入了洞穴，希望能找到埋藏的寶藏。
當隊長正在檢查洞穴入口附近的土壤時，機器顯示地下有黃金。
非常興奮，大家一起挖了一個兩英尺深的坑。
他們終於找到了一枚小金幣，但這枚金幣幾乎毫無價值。
隨後，他們徹底搜查了整個洞穴，但除了一個空鐵皮箱外什麼也沒找到。
儘管如此，許多人仍然相信，“揭示者”很快就會揭示出有價值的東西。

title: Faster Than Sound
titleTranslate: 那輛獲勝的汽車速度有多快？
text:
How fast did the winning car go?
Once a year, a race is held for old cars.
A lot of cars entered for this race last year and there was a great deal of excitement just before it began.
One of the most handsome cars was a Rolls-Royce Silver Ghost.
The most unusual car was a Benz which had only three wheels.
Built in 1885, it was the oldest car taking part.
After a great many loud explosions, the race began.
Many of the cars broke down on the course and some drivers spent more time under their cars than in them!
A few cars, however, completed the race.
The winning car reached a speed of forty miles an hour -- much faster than any of its rivals.
It sped downhill at the end of the race and its driver had a lot of trouble trying to stop it.
The race gave everyone a great deal of pleasure.
It was very different from modern car races but no less exciting.
textTranslate:
那輛獲勝的汽車速度有多快？
每年都會舉辦一場專為老爺車設計的比賽。
去年有很多車報名參加了這場比賽，比賽開始前氣氛十分熱烈。
其中最引人注目的汽車之一是勞斯萊斯銀魅（Rolls-Royce Silver Ghost）。
最不尋常的汽車是一輛只有三個輪子的賓士（Benz）汽車。
這輛汽車建於1885年，是參賽車輛中歷史最悠久的一輛。
在一陣巨大的轟鳴聲後，比賽開始了。
許多汽車在賽道上發生了故障；有些司機花在檢查汽車故障上的時間，甚至比他們實際駕駛汽車的時間還要長！
然而，還是有幾輛車完成了比賽。
獲勝的汽車達到了每小時40英里的速度——這比它的所有競爭對手都要快得多。
比賽結束時，這輛車飛速衝下山坡，駕駛員費了好大勁才把它停下來。
這場比賽給大家帶來了極大的樂趣。
這與現代的汽車比賽非常不同，但同樣令人興奮。

title: Can I Help You, Madam
titleTranslate: 那個女人得到了她想要的東西嗎？
text:
Did the woman get what she wanted?
A woman in jeans stood at the window of an expensive shop.
Though she hesitated for a moment,
she finally went in and asked to see a dress that was in the window.
The assistant who served her did not like the way she was dressed.
Glancing at her scornfully, he told her that the dress was sold.
The woman walked out of the shop angrily and decided to punish the assistant next day.
She returned to the shop the following morning dressed in a fur coat,
with a handbag in one hand and a long umbrella in the other.
After seeking out the rude assistant,
she asked for the same dress.
Not realizing who she was, the assistant was eager to serve her this time.
With great difficulty, he climbed into the shop window to get the dress.
As soon as she saw it, the woman said she did not like it.
She enjoyed herself making the assistant bring almost everything in the window
before finally buying the dress she had first asked for.
textTranslate:
那個女人得到了她想要的東西嗎？
一個穿著牛仔褲的女人站在一家高檔商店的窗前。
雖然她猶豫了一會兒，
她終於走進店裡，要求看一看櫥窗裡的那件連衣裙。
接待她的店員不喜歡她的穿著打扮。
他輕蔑地瞥了她一眼，然後告訴她那條裙子已經賣掉了。
那位女士怒氣衝衝地走出商店，決定第二天要教訓一下那個店員。
第二天早上，她穿著一件皮草大衣回到了店裡。
一隻手拿著手提包，另一隻手拿著一把長傘。
找到那個無禮的店員後，
她又要看同一條裙子。
由於沒認出她是誰，這次店員殷勤地為她服務。
他費了很大的勁才爬進商店的櫥窗裡，去拿那條裙子。
她一看到那件衣服，就說不喜歡。
她樂此不疲地讓店員把櫥窗裡幾乎所有的東西都拿了出來，
最後才買下她最初要看的那條裙子。

title: A Blessing in Disguise
titleTranslate: 為什麼牧師拒絕砍倒那棵樹呢？
text:
Why does the vicar refuse to cut down the tree?
The tiny village of Frinley is said to possess a 'cursed tree'.
Because the tree was mentioned in a newspaper,
the number of visitors to Frinley has now increased.
The tree was planted near the church fifty years ago, but it is only in recent years that it has gained an evil reputation.
It is said that if anyone touches the tree, he will have bad luck; if he picks a leaf, he will die.
Many villagers believe that the tree has already claimed a number of victims.
The vicar has been asked to have the tree cut down, but so far he has refused.
He has pointed out that the tree is a useful source of income,
as tourists have been coming from all parts of the country to see it.
In spite of all that has been said,
the tourists have been picking leaves and cutting their names on the tree-trunk.
So far, not one of them has been struck down by sudden death!
textTranslate:
為什麼牧師拒絕砍倒那棵樹呢？
據說，弗林利這個小村莊裡有一棵“被詛咒的樹”。
因為這棵樹在報紙上被提及後，
來弗林利參觀的遊客數量現在已經增加了。
這棵樹是在五十年前種在教堂附近的，但直到最近幾年，它才獲得了“邪惡”的名聲。
據說，如果有人觸碰這棵樹，就會遭遇厄運；如果他摘下一片葉子，就會死亡。
許多村民認為，這棵樹已經害死了不少人。
牧師被要求把那棵樹砍掉，但他至今仍然拒絕這樣做。
他指出，這棵樹能帶來可觀的收入，
因為遊客們從全國各地紛紛前來參觀它。
儘管有這麼多傳言，
遊客們一直在摘樹葉，並在樹幹上刻下自己的名字。
到目前為止，他們當中還沒有一個人突然死亡！

title: In or Out
titleTranslate: 為什麼雷克斯會逃跑呢？
text:
Why did Rex run away?
Our dog, Rex, used to sit outside our front gate and bark.
Every time he wanted to come into the garden he would bark until someone opened the gate.
As the neighbours complained of the noise, my husband spent weeks training him to press his paw on the latch to let himself in.
Rex soon became an expert at opening the gate.
However, when I was going out shopping last week, I noticed him in the garden near the gate.
This time he was barking so that someone would let him out!
Since then, he has developed another bad habit.
As soon as he opens the gate from the outside, he comes into the garden and waits until the gate shuts.
Then he sits and barks until someone lets him out.
After this he immediately lets himself in and begins barking again.
Yesterday my husband removed the gate and Rex got so annoyed we have not seen him since.
textTranslate:
為什麼雷克斯會逃跑呢？
我們的狗雷克斯過去常常坐在前門外吠叫。
每次他想進入花園時，都會不停地吠叫，直到有人開啟大門。
由於鄰居們抱怨噪音太大，我丈夫花了好幾個星期訓練它用爪子按門閂，好讓它自己進來。
雷克斯很快就掌握了開門的技巧。
然而，上週我去購物的時候，我在大門附近的花園裡看到了他。
這次他大聲吠叫，就是為了有人能放他出去！
從那以後，他又養成了一個壞習慣。
他一從外面開啟大門，就走進花園，然後等待大門關上。
然後他就坐著，不停地叫，直到有人放他出去。
之後，他立刻自己進了門，又開始吠叫起來。
昨天我丈夫把門拆了，雷克斯氣壞了，從那以後我們就再也沒見過它了。

title: The Future
titleTranslate: 貝爾林斯基夫人說的話會成真嗎？
text:
Does what Madam Bellinsky said come true?
At a village fair, I decided to visit a fortune-teller called Madam Bellinsky.
I went into her tent and she told me to sit down.
After I had given her some money, she looked into a crystal ball and said:
'A relation of yours is coming to see you.
She will be arriving this evening and intends to stay for a few days.
The moment you leave this tent, you will get a big surprise.
A woman you know well will rush towards you.
She will speak to you and then she will lead you away from this place. That is all.'
As soon as I went outside, I forgot all about Madam Bellinsky because my wife hurried towards me.
'Where have you been hiding?' she asked impatiently.
'Your sister will be here in less than an hour and we must be at the station to meet her. We are late already.'
As she walked away, I followed her out of the fair.
textTranslate:
貝爾林斯基夫人說的話會成真嗎？
在一個鄉村集市上，我決定去拜訪一位名叫貝林斯基夫人的算命師。
我走進她的帳篷，她讓我坐下。
在我給了她一些錢之後，她看了看那個水晶球，然後說道：
你的一個親戚要來看你。
她將於今晚到達，並打算在這裡待上幾天。
你一離開這個帳篷，就會大吃一驚。
一個你非常熟悉的女人會衝向你。
她會和你說話，然後帶你離開這個地方。就這些。
我一走到外面，就把貝林斯基夫人忘得一乾二淨了，因為我的妻子正朝我匆匆走來。
你跑哪兒去了？她不耐煩地問道。
你妹妹將在一小時內到達，我們必須趕到車站去接她。我們已經遲到了。
她轉身走開時，我便跟著她離開了集市。

title: Trouble with the Hubble
titleTranslate: 望遠鏡在太空中具有怎樣的特殊重要性？
text:
What is the special importance of a telescope in space?
The Hubble telescope was launched into
space by NASA on April 20, 1990 at a cost of over a billion dollars.
Right from the start there was trouble with the Hubble.
The pictures it sent us were very disappointing because its main mirror was faulty!
NASA is now going to put the telescope right,
so it will soon be sending up four astronauts to repair it.
The shuttle Endeavour will be taking the astronauts to the Hubble.
A robot-arm from the Endeavour will grab the telescope and hold it while the astronauts make the necessary repairs.
Of course, the Hubble is above the earth's atmosphere,
so it will soon be sending us the clearest pictures of the stars and distant galaxies that we have ever seen.
The Hubble will tell us a great deal about the age and size of the universe.
By the time you read this, the Hubble's eagle eye will have sent us thousands and thousands of wonderful pictures.
textTranslate:
望遠鏡在太空中具有怎樣的特殊重要性？
哈勃望遠鏡於1990年4月20日由美國國家航空航天局（NASA）發射升空，耗資超過10億美元。
這艘航天器是由美國國家航空航天局（NASA）於1990年4月20日發射的，耗資超過10億美元。
從一開始，哈勃望遠鏡就麻煩不斷。
它發給我們的照片非常令人失望，因為它的主鏡有問題（即主鏡無法正常工作）。
NASA現在將著手修復這臺望遠鏡。
因此，他們很快就會派遣四名宇航員去修理它。
“奮進號”太空梭將把宇航員送往哈勃望遠鏡。
“奮進號”的機械臂會抓住望遠鏡，並在宇航員進行必要維修時將其固定。
當然，哈勃望遠鏡位於地球大氣層之外。
因此，它很快就會向我們傳送迄今為止最清晰的恆星和遙遠星系的影象。
哈勃望遠鏡將幫助我們瞭解宇宙的年齡和規模。
等你讀到這篇文章時，哈勃望遠鏡那“鷹眼”已經為我們傳回了成千上萬張精彩的照片。

title: After the Fire
titleTranslate: 火災過後，村莊面臨著哪些危險？
text:
What was the danger to the villages after the fire?
Firemen had been fighting the forest fire for nearly three weeks before they could get it under control.
A short time before, great trees had covered the countryside for miles around.
Now, smoke still rose up from the warm ground over the desolate hills.
Winter was coming on and the hills threatened the surrounding villages with destruction,
for heavy rain would not only wash away the soil but would cause serious floods as well.
When the fire had at last been put out,
the forest authorities ordered several tons of a special type of grass-seed which would grow quickly.
The seed was sprayed over the ground in huge quantities by aeroplanes.
The planes had been planting seed for nearly a month when it began to rain.
By then, however, in many places the grass had already taken root.
In place of the great trees which had been growing there for centuries,
patches of green had begun to appear in the blackened soil.
textTranslate:
火災過後，村莊面臨著哪些危險？
消防員們與這場森林火災抗爭了近三週，才終於將其控制住。
就在不久之前，這片鄉村的周圍幾英里範圍內，都長滿了高大的樹木。
此刻，煙霧仍從那片荒涼山丘上溫暖的地面中升起。
冬天即將來臨，那些山丘威脅著周圍的村莊，隨時可能將其摧毀。
因為大雨不僅會沖走土壤，還會引發嚴重的洪水。
當大火終於被撲滅後，
森林管理部門訂購了幾噸一種特殊的草籽，這種草籽生長速度非常快。
這些種子被飛機大量噴灑到地面上。
這些飛機已經在進行播種作業近一個月了，就在這時，雨開始下了。
然而，到那時為止，草已經在許多地方生根發芽了。
取而代之的是，那些已經生長了幾個世紀的參天大樹不見了。
在燒焦的土壤中，開始出現了一小塊塊綠色的斑點。

title: She Was Not Amused
titleTranslate: 為什麼珍妮想離開婚禮現場呢？
text:
Why did Jenny want to leave the wedding reception?
Jeremy Hampden has a large circle of friends and is very popular at parties.
Everybody admires him for his great sense of humour--everybody, that is, except his six-year-old daughter, Jenny.
Recently, one of Jeremy's closest friends asked him to make a speech at a wedding reception.
This is the sort of thing that Jeremy loves.
He prepared the speech carefully and went to the wedding with Jenny.
He had included a large number of funny stories in the speech and, of course, it was a great success.
As soon as he had finished Jenny told him she wanted to go home.
Jeremy was a little disappointed by this but he did as his daughter asked.
On the way home, he asked Jenny if she had enjoyed the speech.
To his surprise she said she hadn't.
Jeremy asked her why this was so and she told him that she did not like to see so many people laughing at him!
textTranslate:
為什麼珍妮想離開婚禮現場呢？
傑里米·漢普登擁有很多朋友，在聚會上非常受歡迎。
大家都很欣賞他的幽默感——當然，除了他六歲的女兒珍妮之外。
最近，傑里米的一位密友請求他在一場婚禮招待會上發表演講。
這正是傑里米喜歡做的事情。
他仔細準備了演講稿，然後和珍妮一起參加了婚禮。
他在演講中穿插了許多有趣的故事，當然，這次演講非常成功。
他剛說完，珍妮就告訴他她想回家了。
傑里米對此感到有些失望，但他還是按照女兒的要求做了。
在回家的路上，他問珍妮是否喜歡那場演講。
令他驚訝的是，她說她並不喜歡。
傑里米問她為什麼，她告訴他她不喜歡看到那麼多人嘲笑他！

title: The Channel Tunnel
titleTranslate: 為什麼第一條隧道沒有完成呢？
text:
Why was the first tunnel not completed?
In 1858, a French engineer, Aime Thome de Gamond,
arrived in England with a plan for a 21-mile tunnel under the English Channel.
He said that it would be possible to build a platform in the centre of the Channel.
This platform would serve as a port and a railway station.
The tunnel would be well-ventilated if tall chimneys were built above sea level.
In 1860, a better plan was put forward by an Englishman, William Low.
He suggested that a double railway-tunnel should be built.
This would solve the problem of ventilation for if a train entered this tunnel, it would draw in fresh air behind it.
Forty-two years later a tunnel was actually begun.
If, at the time, the British had not feared invasion, it would have been completed.
The world had to wait almost another 100 years for the Channel Tunnel.
It was officially opened on March 7, 1994, finally connecting Britain to the European continent.
textTranslate:
為什麼第一條隧道沒有完成呢？
1858年，一位名叫艾姆·託姆·德·加蒙（Aime Thome de Gamond）的法國工程師……
他帶著一個在英吉利海峽下建造一條21英里隧道的計劃來到了英格蘭。
他說，在英吉利海峽中央建造一個平臺是可行的。
這個平臺將用作港口和火車站。
如果在海平面上方建造高聳的煙囪，這條隧道將會擁有良好的通風條件。
1860年，一位名叫威廉·洛（William Low）的英國人提出了一個更完善的方案。
他建議建造一條雙線鐵路隧道。
這樣就能解決通風問題，因為如果火車進入這條隧道，它就會把新鮮空氣吸進來。
四十二年後，一條隧道真的開始修建了。
如果當時英國人不擔心遭到入侵的話，這項工程就會完成了。
世界又等待了近100年，才看到了英法海底隧道的建成。
它於1994年3月7日正式啟用，終於將英國與歐洲大陸連線了起來。

title: Jumbo Versus the Police
titleTranslate: 為什麼警察不得不把 Jumbo 從主街上推走？
text:
Why did the police have to push Jumbo off the main street?
Last Christmas, the circus owner, Jimmy Gates, decided to take some presents to a children's hospital.
Dressed up as Father Christmas and accompanied by a 'guard of honour' of six pretty girls,
he set off down the main street of the city riding a baby elephant called Jumbo.
He should have known that the police would never allow this sort of thing.
A policeman approached Jimmy and told him he ought to have gone along a side street as Jumbo was holding up the traffic.
Though Jimmy agreed to go at once, Jumbo refused to move.
Fifteen policemen had to push very hard to get him off the main street.
The police had a difficult time, but they were most amused.
'Jumbo must weigh a few tons,' said a policeman afterwards,
'so it was fortunate that we didn't have to carry him.
Of course, we should arrest him, but as he has a good record, we shall let him off this time.'
textTranslate:
為什麼警察不得不把 Jumbo 從主街上推走？
去年聖誕節，馬戲團老闆吉米·蓋茨決定把一些禮物送到兒童醫院去。
他裝扮成聖誕老人，由六位漂亮的女孩組成的“儀仗隊”陪同著他。
他騎著一隻名叫“Jumbo”的小象，沿著城市的主街道出發了。
他本應該知道，警察絕不會允許這種事情發生的。
一名警察走過來對吉米說：“你應該走小路，因為‘Jumbo’正在阻礙交通。”
雖然吉米立刻同意離開，但“Jumbo”卻拒絕移動。
十五名警察費了很大勁才把他從主街上弄走。
警察們遇到了不少困難，不過他們覺得這件事非常有趣（或者說：雖然過程很艱難，但他們覺得這件事很有趣）。
“Jumbo肯定有好幾噸重，”一名警察事後說道，
“所以我們很幸運，不必抬著他走。
“當然，我們應該逮捕他，但鑑於他表現良好，這次我們就放他一馬吧。

title: Sweet as Honey
titleTranslate: 什麼東西“甜如蜜”？為什麼這麼說呢？
text:
What was 'sweet as honey' and why?
In 1963 a Lancaster bomber crashed on Wallis Island,
a remote place in the South Pacific, a long way west of Samoa.
The plane wasn't too badly damaged,
but over the years the crash was forgotten and the wreck remained undisturbed.
Then in 1989, twenty-six years after the crash, the plane was accidentally rediscovered in an aerial survey of the island.
By this time, a Lancaster bomber in reasonable condition was rare and worth rescuing.
The French authorities had the plane packaged and moved in parts back to France.
Now a group of enthusiasts are going to have the plane restored.
It has four Rolls-Royce Merlin engines but the group will need to have only three of them rebuilt.
Imagine their surprise and delight when they broke open the packing cases
and found that the fourth engine was sweet as honey--still in perfect condition.
A colony of bees had turned the engine into a hive and it was totally preserved in beeswax!
textTranslate:
什麼東西“甜如蜜”？為什麼這麼說呢？
1963年，一架蘭開斯特轟炸機在沃利斯島上墜毀了。
一個位於南太平洋的偏遠之地，在薩摩亞以西很遠的地方。
飛機的損壞程度並不嚴重。
然而，隨著時間的推移，這場墜機事故被人們遺忘了，飛機殘骸也一直無人打擾。
然後在1989年，也就是飛機失事26年後，這架飛機在一次對該島嶼的航空勘測中被意外重新發現了。
到了這個時候，一架狀況良好的蘭開斯特轟炸機已經非常稀少，值得搶救。
法國當局將飛機打包，分部件運回了法國。
現在，一群愛好者正著手修復這架飛機。
這架飛機有四臺羅爾斯-羅伊斯“梅林”發動機，但這群人只需要重建其中三臺。
想象一下，當他們開啟包裝箱時，他們的驚訝和喜悅之情吧！
結果發現第四臺發動機“甜如蜜”——狀況依然完好。
一群蜜蜂將那臺發動機改造成了一個蜂巢，並且整個發動機都被完美地儲存在了蜂蠟之中！

title: Volcanoes
titleTranslate: 為什麼塔齊夫要這樣冒生命危險呢？
text:
Why does Tazieff risk his life like this?
Haroun Tazieff, the Polish scientist, has spent his lifetime studying active volcanoes and deep caves in all parts of the world.
In 1948, he went to Lake Kivu in the Congo to observe a new volcano which he later named Kituro.
Tazieff was able to set up his camp very close to the volcano while it was erupting violently.
Though he managed to take a number of brilliant photographs, he could not stay near the volcano for very long.
He noticed that a river of liquid rock was coming towards him.
It threatened to surround him completely, but Tazieff managed to escape just in time.
He waited until the volcano became quiet and he was able to return two days later.
This time, he managed to climb into the mouth of Kituro so that he could take photographs and measure temperatures.
Tazieff has often risked his life in this way.
He has been able to tell us more about active volcanoes than any man alive.
textTranslate:
為什麼塔齊夫要這樣冒生命危險呢？
波蘭科學家哈魯恩·塔齊夫（Haroun Tazieff）畢生致力於研究世界各地的活火山和深洞穴。
1948年，他前往剛果的基伍湖（Lake Kivu），觀察一座新發現的火山，並將其命名為“基圖羅”（Kituro）。
塔齊夫能夠在火山劇烈噴發的時候，將他的營地搭建得離火山非常近。
儘管他拍到了許多精彩的照片，但他卻無法在火山附近停留太久。
他注意到一股熔岩流正朝他湧來。
巖流眼看就要將他完全包圍，但塔齊夫及時脫險了。
他一直等到火山平息下來，兩天後才得以返回。
這一次，他設法爬進了基圖羅火山口，以便拍照和測量溫度。
塔齊夫經常以這種方式冒著生命危險。
他比在世的任何人都更瞭解活火山的相關情況，能夠向我們提供更多關於活火山的詳細資訊。

title: Persistent
titleTranslate: 為什麼伊麗莎白告訴奈傑爾她要去看牙醫？
text:
Why did Elizabeth tell Nigel that she was going to the dentist?
I crossed the street to avoid meeting him but he saw me and came running towards me.
It was no use pretending that I had not seen him, so I waved to him.
I never enjoy meeting Nigel Dykes.
He never has anything to do.
No matter how busy you are, he always insists on coming with you.
I had to think of a way of preventing him from following me around all morning.
'Hello, Nigel,' I said 'Fancy meeting you here!'
'Hi, Elizabeth,' Nigel answered. 'I was just wondering how to spend the morning--until I saw you.
You're not busy doing anything, are you?'
'No, not at all,' I answered. 'I'm going to...'
'Would you mind my coming with you?' he asked, before I had finished speaking.
'Not at all,' I lied, 'but I'm going to the dentist.'
'Then I'll come with you,' he answered.
'There's always plenty to read in the waiting room!'
textTranslate:
為什麼伊麗莎白告訴奈傑爾她要去看牙醫？
我穿過馬路想避開他，但他看見了我，就朝我跑了過來。
假裝沒看見他是沒用的，所以我向他揮了揮手。
我從來都不喜歡見到尼格爾·戴克斯。
他總是無所事事。
無論你有多忙，他總是堅持要跟著你。
我得想辦法阻止他一整個早上都跟著我。
“你好，尼格爾，”我說，“真沒想到在這裡遇見你！”
“嗨，伊麗莎白，”奈傑爾回答，“我正琢磨著怎麼打發這個上午——直到看見了你。
“你現在不忙，對吧？”
“不忙，”我回答，“我正要去……”
“你介意我陪你一起去嗎？”他還沒等我說完就問了。
“當然不介意，”我撒謊說，“不過我要去看牙醫。
“那我就跟你一起去，”他回答。
“候診室裡總有好多東西可以看！”

title: But not Murder
titleTranslate: 你認為那位作者透過了駕駛考試嗎？為什麼？
text:
Do you think that the writer passed his driving test? Why?
I was being tested for a driving licence for the third time.
I had been asked to drive in heavy traffic and had done so successfully.
After having been instructed to drive out of town, I began to acquire confidence.
Sure that I had passed, I was almost beginning to enjoy my test.
The examiner must have been pleased with my performance, for he smiled and said, 'Just one more thing Mr. Eames.
Let us suppose that a child suddenly crosses the road in front of you.
As soon as I tap on the window, you must stop within five feet.'
I continued driving and after some time, the examiner tapped loudly.
Though the sound could be heard clearly, it took me a long time to react.
I suddenly pressed the brake pedal hard and we were both thrown forward.
The examiner looked at me sadly.
'Mr. Eames,' he said, in a mournful voice, 'you have just killed that child!'
textTranslate:
你認為那位作者透過了駕駛考試嗎？為什麼？
這是我第三次參加駕照考試了。
我曾被要求在繁忙的交通中駕駛，而且我成功地做到了。
在接到“開車離開城鎮”的指令後，我開始有了信心。
當我確定自己已經透過了考試後，我甚至開始享受這個考試過程了。
考官一定對我的表現很滿意，因為他微笑著對我說：“還有一件事，埃姆斯先生。”
假設突然有一個孩子在你面前橫穿馬路。
“我一敲窗戶，你就必須在五英尺內停下來。”
我繼續開車，過了一會兒，考官大聲地敲了敲車窗。
雖然那個聲音聽得非常清楚，但我還是花了很長時間才做出反應。
我突然猛踩剎車，結果我們倆都被向前甩了出去。
考官難過地看著我。
“埃姆斯先生，”他用悲傷的語氣說道，“你剛剛撞死了那個孩子！”

title: Red for Danger
titleTranslate: 那個醉漢是如何被從鬥牛場裡帶走的呢？
text:
How was the drunk removed from the ring?
During a bullfight, a drunk suddenly wandered into the middle of the ring.
The crowd began to shout, but the drunk was unaware of the danger.
The bull was busy with the matador at the time,
but it suddenly caught sight of the drunk who was shouting rude remarks and waving a red cap.
Apparently sensitive to criticism,
the bull forgot all about the matador and charged at the drunk.
The crowd suddenly grew quiet.
The drunk, however, seemed quite sure of himself.
When the bull got close to him, he clumsily stepped aside to let it pass.
The crowd broke into cheers and the drunk bowed.
By this time, however three men had come into the ring and they quickly dragged the drunk to safety.
Even the bull seemed to feel sorry for him,
for it looked on sympathetically until the drunk was out of the way before once more turning its attention to the matador.
textTranslate:
那個醉漢是如何被從鬥牛場裡帶走的呢？
在一場鬥牛比賽中，一個醉漢突然走進了鬥牛場的中央。
人群開始大聲呼喊，但醉漢並沒有意識到危險。
當時公牛正與鬥牛士搏鬥，
突然它注意到了那個醉漢——醉漢正在大聲說髒話，並揮舞著一頂紅色帽子。
顯然，這頭公牛對批評很敏感，
它立刻忘記了鬥牛士的存在，衝向了醉漢。
人群頓時安靜了下來。
然而，醉漢似乎對自己很有信心。
當公牛靠近時，他笨拙地躲到一邊，讓公牛從自己身邊經過。
人群爆發出歡呼聲，醉漢也鞠了一躬。
然而這時，已經有三個人走進了鬥牛場，他們迅速把醉漢拖到了安全的地方。
就連那頭公牛似乎也對他感到同情。
它同情地看著那個醉漢，直到他被帶走後，才再次把注意力轉向鬥牛士。

title: A Famous Clock
titleTranslate: 大本鐘曾經出過故障嗎？
text:
Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?
When you visit London, one of the first things you will see is Big Ben,
the famous clock which can be heard all over the world on the B.B.C.
If the Houses of Parliament had not been burned down in 1834,
the great clock would never have been erected.
Big Ben takes its name from Sir Benjamin Hall who was responsible for the making of the clock when the new Houses of Parliament were being built.
It is not only of immense size, but is extremely accurate as well.
Officials from Greenwich Observatory have the clock checked twice a day.
On the B.B.C. you can hear the clock when it is actually striking because microphones are connected to the clock tower.
Big Ben has rarely gone wrong.
Once, however, it failed to give the correct time.
A painter who had been working on the tower hung a pot of paint on one of the hands and slowed it down!
textTranslate:
大本鐘曾經出過故障嗎？
當你遊覽倫敦時，首先會看到的大概就是大本鐘（Big Ben）了。
那座著名的鐘，它的鐘聲可以透過英國廣播公司（BBC）的廣播傳遍全世界。
如果英國議會大廈沒有在1834年被燒燬的話……
那座大鐘就永遠也不會被建造起來了。
大本鐘的名字來源於本傑明·霍爾爵士（Sir Benjamin Hall）——他在英國新議會大廈建造期間負責這座鐘的製造工作。
它不僅體積龐大，而且精度極高。
格林尼治天文臺的官員每天會檢查這臺時鐘兩次。
在BBC的廣播中，你可以聽到鐘聲的實際敲擊聲——因為麥克風是直接連線到鐘樓的。
大本鐘幾乎從未出過故障。
然而，有一次它沒能準確地顯示時間。
一位在鐘塔上工作的油漆工，把一罐油漆掛在了一根指標上，結果使鍾走慢了！

title: A Car Called Bluebird
titleTranslate: 出了什麼差錯？
text:
What mistake was made?
The great racing driver, Sir Malcolm Campbell, was the first man to drive at over 300 miles per hour.
He set up a new world record in September 1935 at Bonneville Salt Flats, Utah.
Bluebird, the car he was driving, had been specially built for him.
It was over 30 feet in length and had a 2, 500-horsepower engine.
Although Campbell reached a speed of over 304 miles per hour,
he had great difficulty in controlling the car because a tyre burst during the first run.
After his attempt, Campbell was disappointed to learn that his average speed had been 299 miles per hour.
However, a few days later, he was told that a mistake had been made.
His average speed had been 301 miles per hour.
Since that time, racing drivers have reached speeds over 600 miles an hour.
Following in his father's footsteps many years later, Sir Malcolm's son, Donald, also set up a world record.
Like his father, he was driving a car called Bluebird.
textTranslate:
出了什麼差錯？
偉大的賽車手馬爾科姆·坎貝爾爵士（Sir Malcolm Campbell）是第一個駕駛汽車時速超過300英里的人。
他在1935年9月於猶他州的博納維爾鹽灘（Bonneville Salt Flats）創造了新的世界紀錄。
他駕駛的那輛名為“Bluebird”的汽車，是專門為他定製製造的。
它的長度超過了30英尺（約9米），配備了一臺功率為2500馬力的發動機。
儘管坎貝爾的速度超過了每小時304英里，
他在控制汽車時遇到了很大的困難，因為在第一次試跑中一個輪胎爆了。
嘗試之後，坎貝爾得知自己的平均速度僅為每小時299英里，這讓他感到非常失望。
然而，幾天後他被告知，原來出現了一個錯誤。
他的平均速度是每小時301英里。
從那時起，賽車手的行駛速度已經超過了每小時600英里。
多年以後，馬爾科姆爵士的兒子唐納德追隨父親的足跡，也創造了一項世界紀錄。
和他父親一樣，他駕駛著一輛名叫“Bluebird”的汽車。

title: The Record-Holder
titleTranslate: 那個男孩去他想去的地方了嗎？
text:
Did the boy go where he wanted to?
Children who play truant from school are unimaginative.
A quiet days' fishing,
or eight hours in a cinema seeing the same film over and over again, is usually as far as they get.
